file.content:
**About/vouch always-airgap is a security property**: verification can never be influenced by a compromised public registry, even for tether vessels.

==== Implemented: Cloud-Dispatched Tool-Plane Deletes

GAR image-package deletes — `banish` (whole Lode) and `abjure` (every package under a hallmark subtree) — are **cloud-dispatched**, not host-issued.
The workstation submits a Cloud Build and blocks until terminal (conjure-shaped); the in-pool step issues GAR `packages.delete`, polls the returned long-running operation to terminal, and verifies package absence.
The build's outcome IS the delete outcome, which closes a real correctness gap: a host-issued `packages.delete` returns HTTP 200 immediately and the prior code trusted that 200, but the actual deletion runs asynchronously as an LRO whose terminal verdict the workstation never saw (the trust-200 LRO gap).

**The cinched line is tool-plane versus control-plane.**
Only GAR *package* deletes move cloud-side: `banish` and `abjure`.
Control-plane deletes — service accounts, projects, the depot, liens, GCS buckets — stay host-issued REST, as do the member/tag-grain maintenance verbs (the raw image backdoor), which must not depend on the pipeline they exist to clean up after.
The recorded canon is the line itself, not a trajectory toward all-deletes-go-cloud.

content.text:
Review complete, residue fixed and committed (`d49fb3fa2`). Here's the full verdict, by their questions:

**Overall: the architecture is sound and the empirical finding is genuine — I validate it.** The convergence mechanism matches GAR's documented parent-before-child behavior, the gcr-cleaner `--skip-errors` precedent is the right tell, and `rbgjl06` is a clean implementation of "absence is the only truth." But the pace landed with a half-finished prose layer: **seven sites described superseded mechanisms as current** — four shell comments and three spec step-bodies still said "polls each LRO to terminal," and two of the shell comments cited the *disproven* code-5 NOT_FOUND membrane as the live design, pointing at an RBSCB that now says the opposite. RBSAA and RBSLB literally contradicted their own completion notes within the same file. I've fixed all seven; the code itself needed nothing.

**Q1 — cross-family structure: acceptable, and the hazard is self-diagnosing.** The "never co-furnished" assumption isn't a silent convention — both cross-sourced files carry inclusion guards that `buc_die` on a double-source, so a future co-furnishing process fails loudly at source time with an obvious fix (hoist the spine to the rbfc family properly). That bounds the maintenance hazard to "blocks a future that doesn't exist yet, with a clear error." The real debt is the name/coupling mismatch: the spine's functions say `zrbld_`, its sentinel says `zrbfc_`, its file lives in rbld. Relocating to rbfc (`rbfcs_`?) is the clean end-state *if a third rider appears* — not worth the rename churn for two riders. Leave it.

**Q2 — convergence robustness: sound for today's shapes, with one genuine flaw and one budget edge.** The flaw: the 180s per-package deadline is wrong-shaped for very large webs. `fire_delete` is synchronous HTTP (~100–300ms each), so a 1000-version package's *first round alone* can exceed 180s — the deadline then kills a delete that was converging monotonically. Fix when it matters: make the deadline progress-aware (die only when a round removes nothing) or scale it by initial version count. The budget edge: worst case is N×180s in-pool against the 2700s build timeout — 15 pathological packages. The host side is correctly ordered (600 polls × 5s = 3000s > 2700s), so the build always dies first and the host sees a terminal FAILURE, never a split-brain. Fire-and-forget is sound: a systemic failure (403/429) surfaces as deadline-die with every per-round error logged in the build trail — loud and attributable. Two small inconsistencies: `fire_delete` tolerates `HTTPError` but a transient `URLError` (TCP reset — morally a 503) crashes the build; and the urllib calls carry no timeout, so a hung socket stalls until the build timeout. Both cheap hardening, neither urgent.

**Q3 — self-actAs: clean; the smell is next to it.** The grant adds zero capability — the Director workstation already holds the SA's *key* and can mint tokens at will; actAs-on-self is just the Cloud Build API's submission formality. The real finding: **the delete build runs a floating unpinned builder (`gcloud:latest`) under the most GAR-privileged identity in the system.** Every prior floating-builder build (underpin/wsl) ran as writer-only Mason — that asymmetry was the design. A poisoned `:latest` now executes with repoAdmin delete over the whole repo, and the metadata server hands it Director tokens. The bootstrap-pin itch escalates from hygiene to priority *for this builder specifically* — pin `ZRBFC_DELETE_BUILDER` by digest (cheap, preserves the reliquary-less property). One small grace note: the new self-actAs binding lives on the SA's own policy, so divest leaves no tombstone — it dies with the SA.

**Q4 — rbgjl06 is clean.** No code-5 branch, no LRO polling, no residue. The residue was entirely in the description layer (the seven sites, now fixed). The ₢BHAAZ docket also carries the disproven premise in its Cinched section — that's historical instruction text, properly handled by the wrap summary recording the deviation, not by retro-editing.

**Q5 — smoke coverage: adequate for the happy path; two real gaps.** First, **fresh-invest actAs propagation**: the new self-actAs grant joins the same IAM-propagation class as the memo-20260513/20260604 races — the first delete build after a pristine invest may flap at `builds.create`. The next pace's service run uses the standing depot and *won't* catch this; only a pristine-depot gauntlet will. Worth a Class-C tolerance check on the submit path before that gauntlet runs. Second, the failure path has never executed — nobody has seen what a deadline-die or build-FAILURE looks like host-side. The next pace's service-tier run correctly covers the banish/abjure legs for the convergence happy path.

One cross-heat note for the ledger: this pace added two more credential-manifold sites — `rbldd_Delete.sh` sources the Director RBRA in a subshell to extract `CLIENT_EMAIL`, and `zrbfc_wait_build_completion` hardcodes the Director RBRA for poll tokens. Both are exactly what ₣BZ's accessor seam will absorb; no action now, but the seam pace should sweep them.

**Wrap guidance:** with the residue committed, I'd call ₢BHAAZ wrappable. Suggested wrap summary should record the mechanism deviation explicitly — the docket prescribed an honest per-package LRO poll; the live probe disproved the premise it rested on, and convergence (fire + absence-poll) landed instead, live-proven. The floating-builder-under-Director pin and the progress-aware deadline are itch/RBSHR material, not blockers.

input.content:
# ₣BH Fable Recommendation — Pin the Delete Builder by Digest

Date: 2026-06-10

Status: Recommendation from the Fable review of the cloud-dispatch delete architecture
(commit 4f8a5c703). Not a blocker for the landed pace; priority hardening.

## The correctable behavior

`ZRBFC_DELETE_BUILDER="gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest"` — minted in `zrbfc_kindle`
(`Tools/rbk/rbfc0_FoundryCore.sh`) — is a floating, unpinned builder image, and the delete
build runs **as the Director SA** (`zrbld_cloud_delete_dispatch`, `Tools/rbk/rbldd_Delete.sh`),
the most GAR-privileged identity in the system (`repoAdmin`, delete over the whole repository).

Every prior floating-builder use (underpin/wsl gcrane bootstrap) runs as **Mason, writer-only**
— that asymmetry was the design: the identity that executes unpinned or untrusted bytes never
holds delete authority. The delete build breaks the asymmetry: a poisoned `:latest` executes
with delete authority, and the in-pool metadata server hands it Director access tokens
(`metadata_token()` in `Tools/rbk/rbgjl/rbgjl06-package-delete.py` demonstrates the fetch).

The comment on the constant already cites the bootstrap-pin itch (RBS0 `rbsk_pinning_boundary`)
as accepted. The Director-identity pairing escalates that itch from hygiene to priority **for
this builder specifically**.

## Recommended repair

Pin `ZRBFC_DELETE_BUILDER` by digest:
`gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud@sha256:<digest>`, resolved once by hand (or by a small
`gcrane digest` gesture) and recorded in the constant with a dated comment.

- Preserves the reliquary-less property the delete build requires (a digest pin needs no
  reliquary; the delete of the last reliquary still works).
- The step's needs are frozen (python3 + urllib + json), so staleness cost is near zero;
  refresh the digest deliberately, as a reviewed one-line change, rather than riding `:latest`.
- Update the RBSCB "Cloud-Dispatched Tool-Plane Deletes" posture section to record the pin
  when done (it currently inherits the floating-bootstrap framing).

# ₣BH Fable Recommendation — Progress-Aware Convergence Deadline

Date: 2026-06-10

Status: Recommendation from the Fable review of the cloud-dispatch delete architecture
(commit 4f8a5c703). Latent for today's package shapes; wrong-shaped for large webs.

## The correctable behavior

`converge_delete` (`Tools/rbk/rbgjl/rbgjl06-package-delete.py`) enforces a **fixed**
per-package ceiling, `DELETE_DEADLINE_SEC = 180`, measured from the package's start.

`fire_delete` is synchronous HTTP (~100–300ms per call), so one round over a large web costs
roughly `versions × latency`. A ≥1000-version package can spend its entire 180s inside
**round 1** — still making monotonic progress — and then die at the deadline check with
"delete did not converge," killing a delete that was converging. Today's largest observed web
(55 versions, ~11–16s/round) is far from the edge, but the deadline shape penalizes exactly
the packages that most need convergence.

Related budget edge, same shape: worst case is `N_packages × 180s` sequential in-pool
(`main()` loops `converge_delete`), against `RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=2700s` — 15 pathological
packages exceeds the build timeout. (Host-side ordering is correct and should be preserved:
`ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_DELETE=600 × 5s = 3000s > 2700s`, so the build always dies first
and the host sees a clean terminal FAILURE.)

## Recommended repair

Make the deadline progress-aware rather than purely chronological, in `converge_delete`:

- Track the remaining-version count per round (`list_version_ids` already supplies it).
- Die only when a round completes with **no reduction** in remaining count — two consecutive
  no-progress rounds is a reasonable trigger (mirrors the loop-until-dry shape).
- Retain an absolute ceiling as the backstop, scaled to the work:
  e.g. `max(180, initial_versions × 1s + 120)` — never unbounded.

Document the abjure-size budget in `zrbld_cloud_delete_dispatch` (`Tools/rbk/rbldd_Delete.sh`)
or RBSCB: per-package ceilings must sum inside `RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT`; beyond that, either raise
the build timeout for delete builds specifically or split the dispatch into batched builds.

# ₣BH Fable Recommendation — Tolerate URLError in fire_delete

Date: 2026-06-10

Status: Recommendation from the Fable review of the cloud-dispatch delete architecture
(commit 4f8a5c703). Small consistency repair in the convergence step.

## The correctable behavior

`fire_delete` (`Tools/rbk/rbgjl/rbgjl06-package-delete.py`) catches only
`urllib.error.HTTPError`. A transient **`urllib.error.URLError`** — connection reset, DNS
blip, broken pipe — propagates uncaught, crashes the step with a traceback, and fails the
whole build.

That is inconsistent with the step's own philosophy: a TCP reset is morally the same event as
a 503, and the design explicitly declares that no single call's verdict matters — "absence is
the only truth; per-call delete errors are logged for the build trail, never branched on."
The convergence loop exists to absorb exactly this class of transient; the catch clause
absorbs only half of it.

## Recommended repair

In `fire_delete`, catch `urllib.error.URLError` alongside `HTTPError` (note `HTTPError` is a
subclass of `URLError`, so order or structure the handler accordingly), log it in the same
`(reconciling via absence poll)` form, and continue. The next round retries; the deadline
remains the failsafe.

**Deliberately do not extend this tolerance to the truth-readers**: `package_absent` and
`list_version_ids` should stay fail-fast (die loud on anything unexpected), because they are
the arbiters the convergence rests on — a flaky reader silently misreporting absence is the
one failure the design must never absorb. If their fail-fast proves noisy under real network
weather, a single bounded retry is the most they should gain.

# ₣BH Fable Recommendation — Bound urllib Calls with a Socket Timeout

Date: 2026-06-10

Status: Recommendation from the Fable review of the cloud-dispatch delete architecture
(commit 4f8a5c703). Cheap hang-bounding in the convergence step.

## The correctable behavior

Every `urllib.request.urlopen` call in `Tools/rbk/rbgjl/rbgjl06-package-delete.py` —
`metadata_token()` and `gar_fetch()` (which carries `package_absent`, `list_version_ids`,
and `fire_delete`) — passes **no timeout**. Python's default is `None`: an unbounded block
on a hung socket.

The failsafes that eventually fire are the wrong grain: a single hung GET stalls the entire
delete until the Cloud Build timeout (`RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=2700s`) or the host poll ceiling
kills the build — turning one stuck connection into a full-build failure with no log line
naming the stall.

## Recommended repair

Pass an explicit timeout to `urlopen` in `gar_fetch` and `metadata_token` — 30s is generous
for both the metadata server and GAR REST.

Classify the resulting `socket.timeout` / `TimeoutError` by caller, consistent with the
URLError-tolerance recommendation (companion memo):

- In `fire_delete`: tolerated and logged like any other per-call error — the absence poll
  arbitrates.
- In the truth-readers (`package_absent`, `list_version_ids`) and `metadata_token`: die loud
  (or one bounded retry) — a timed-out read names its stall in the build log instead of
  freezing silently to the build timeout.

# ₣BH Fable Recommendation — Self-actAs Propagation Flap at Delete-Build Submit

Date: 2026-06-10

Status: Recommendation from the Fable review of the cloud-dispatch delete architecture
(commit 4f8a5c703). Not observable on a standing depot; will surface on a pristine gauntlet.

## The correctable behavior

`rbgg_invest_director` (`Tools/rbk/rbgg_Governor.sh`) now grants the Director SA
`roles/iam.serviceAccountUser` **on itself** — the actAs binding that `builds.create` checks
when the Director-authenticated workstation submits a build whose run-as identity is the
Director SA (`zrbld_spine_dispatch`, `Tools/rbk/rblds_Spine.sh`).

This binding joins the known IAM-propagation race class (Class-C;
memo-20260513-iam-propagation-race-director-invest-gar,
memo-20260604-credential-churn-leak-and-propagation-races): freshly granted, it can lag.
The first delete build submitted shortly after a pristine invest may fail `builds.create`
with PERMISSION_DENIED on the actAs check — a flap, not a defect, but the submit path has no
tolerance for it: `zrbld_spine_dispatch` does `rbuh_json POST` + `rbuh_require_ok` and dies
on the first refusal.

The live smoke (depot canest3bhm100001) ran against a standing depot with long-settled
grants, so this path is **unexercised**. The service-tier verification pace also runs on the
standing depot and will not catch it; only a pristine-depot gauntlet (invest → promptly
banish/abjure) will.

## Recommended repair

Pick one (the first is cheaper and matches existing patterns):

1. **Invest-side read-back gate** — extend the rbk-08 read-back-verification pattern (RBSHR
   "Read-back verification universalization") to the self-actAs binding: after the grant in
   `rbgg_invest_director`, poll the SA IAM policy until the binding is visible before
   declaring invest complete. Confines the wait to invest, keeps the submit path simple.
2. **Submit-side Class-C tolerance** — in `zrbld_spine_dispatch` (or a delete-specific
   wrapper in `zrbld_cloud_delete_dispatch`), bounded retry on the specific
   PERMISSION_DENIED-actAs signature, mirroring the ~81s Class-C tolerance shape. Broader
   blast radius (touches all spine riders), so prefer (1).

Either way, characterize the live signature on the first pristine gauntlet rather than
guessing its exact error body — the lesson of the code-5/code-9 correction this same pace
just taught.

content.text:
Five memos written and notched against ₣BH (`b2fbcfb19`):

1. **`pin-delete-builder`** — `ZRBFC_DELETE_BUILDER` floating `:latest` under the Director identity; pin by digest, update RBSCB.
2. **`convergence-deadline-shape`** — fixed 180s deadline in `converge_delete` can kill a monotonically-converging large web; make it progress-aware (die on no-progress rounds) with a scaled ceiling, and document the N×180s vs 2700s build-timeout budget.
3. **`urlerror-tolerance`** — `fire_delete` tolerates `HTTPError` but crashes on `URLError`; tolerate both, keep the truth-readers fail-fast.
4. **`urllib-timeout`** — no socket timeout anywhere in `rbgjl06`; add 30s, classified per caller.
5. **`actas-propagation-flap`** — the new self-actAs grant joins the Class-C propagation race; unexercisable on a standing depot, will surface on a pristine gauntlet. Recommended repair is the invest-side read-back gate (the rbk-08 pattern), with the explicit note to characterize the live signature first rather than guess it — the code-5/code-9 lesson applied to itself.

Each memo names the functions and files, the recommended repair, and what deliberately *not* to change (e.g., the truth-readers stay loud; the host-outlasts-build poll ordering is correct and should be preserved). They're sized to slate as paces directly if you want them in the heat, or to hand to the Opus session as pre-wrap or follow-on work — none are blockers for wrapping ₢BHAAZ.

17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Lode - lifecycle REST (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
20	#   divine — enumerate every Lode by touchmark (read-only)
21	#   augur  — inspect one Lode: member tags + decoded rbi_vouch envelope (read-only)
22	#   banish — delete a whole Lode (Director credentials)
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	
26	######################################################################
27	# External Functions (rbld_*)
28	
29	rbld_divine() {
30	  zrbld_sentinel
31	
32	  buc_doc_brief "Divine Lodes — enumerate every Lode by touchmark (read-only)"
33	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
34	
35	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
36	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
37	    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
38	  local z_token=""
39	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
40	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
41	
42	  buc_step "Enumerating Lodes under ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/"
43	  zrbfc_list_anchors_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}"
44	
45	  if ! test -s "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"; then
46	    buc_info "No Lodes found under ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/"
47	    buc_success "Divine complete — 0 Lodes"
48	    return 0
49	  fi
50	
51	  # Kind-letter legend, printed once so rows carry no repeated per-row column.
52	  # A touchmark's leading letter is its kind (b260602075327 -> bole); the
53	  # reader decodes the prefix from this key. One entry per implemented kind.
54	  local -r z_kind_fmt="    %-3s %-10s %s\n"
55	  echo ""
56	  printf "  Kinds (touchmark prefix):\n"
57	  printf "${z_kind_fmt}" "${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}"      "bole"      "upstream OCI image, consumed as a FROM line"
58	  printf "${z_kind_fmt}" "${RBGC_LODE_KIND_RELIQUARY}" "reliquary" "date-cohort of build-tool images"
59	  printf "${z_kind_fmt}" "${RBGC_LODE_KIND_WSL}"       "wsl"       "vendor-published rootfs tarball, opaque-blob member"
60	
61	  # Load the touchmark list fully before iterating: the per-Lode tags fetch
62	  # spawns curl (via rbuh), and a child touching stdin would consume the
63	  # loop's remaining input. Load-then-iterate keeps that FD closed.
64	  local z_touchmarks=()
65	  local z_touch=""
66	  while IFS= read -r z_touch || test -n "${z_touch}"; do
67	    test -n "${z_touch}" || continue
68	    z_touchmarks+=("${z_touch}")
69	  done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"
70	
71	  local -r z_row_fmt="  %-15s %s\n"
72	  echo ""
73	  printf "${z_row_fmt}" "TOUCHMARK" "IMAGE"
74	  printf "${z_row_fmt}" "---------------" "--------------------------------------"
75	
76	  local z_idx=0
77	  local z_pkg=""
78	  local z_pkg_encoded=""
79	  local z_tags_url=""
80	  local z_enum_infix=""
81	  local z_resp_file=""
82	  local z_image_file=""
83	  local z_image=""
84	  for z_idx in "${!z_touchmarks[@]}"; do
85	    z_touch="${z_touchmarks[$z_idx]}"
86	
87	    # One tags-list per Lode. The IMAGE column adapts to the Lode's member
88	    # scheme (the touchmark prefix names the kind; see the legend above). A
89	    # single-image Lode (bole) shows its unsprued fingerprint tag
90	    # <sanitized-origin>-<sha10>, located via the sha10 taken from the
91	    # rbi_sha256-<hex> member tag so Director semantic names (also unsprued)
92	    # cannot masquerade as the fingerprint. A clean-scheme cohort (reliquary)
93	    # carries no digest/fingerprint layer, so it has no single fingerprint to
94	    # show — report its member count instead (every non-:rbi_vouch tag is a
95	    # member). Per-Lode infix preserves each response for forensics.
96	    z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touch}"
97	    z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
98	    z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1000"
99	    z_enum_infix="rbld_divine_enum_${z_idx}"
100	    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_enum_infix}"
101	    rbuh_require_ok "List tags for Lode ${z_touch}" "${z_enum_infix}"
102	    z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_enum_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
103	
104	    z_image_file="${ZRBLD_DIVINE_PREFIX}enum_${z_idx}_image.txt"
105	    jq -r --arg dp "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" --arg vouch "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" '
106	      [.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")] as $names
107	      | ([$names[] | select(startswith($dp)) | ltrimstr($dp)[0:10]][0]) as $sha10
108	      | ([$names[] | select((startswith("rbi_") | not) and ($sha10 != null) and endswith("-" + $sha10))][0]) as $fingerprint
109	      | if $fingerprint != null then $fingerprint
110	        else "(cohort: \([$names[] | select(. != $vouch)] | length) members)"
111	        end
112	    ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_image_file}" \
113	      || buc_die "Failed to summarize Lode ${z_touch}"
114	    z_image=$(<"${z_image_file}")
115	    test -n "${z_image}" || buc_die "Empty summary extraction for Lode ${z_touch}"
116	
117	    printf "${z_row_fmt}" "${z_touch}" "${z_image}"
118	  done
119	  echo ""
120	  buc_info "Total Lodes: ${#z_touchmarks[@]}"
121	  buc_success "Divine complete"
122	}
123

144	  local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"

153	  rbuh_require_ok "List tags for Lode ${z_touchmark}" "${z_tags_infix}"
154	
155	  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_tags_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
156	  local -r z_tags_file="${ZRBLD_AUGUR_PREFIX}tags.txt"

158	    || buc_die "Failed to extract member tags for Lode ${z_touchmark}"
159	  test -s "${z_tags_file}" \
160	    || buc_die "No member tags found under ${z_pkg} — Lode not present in registry"
161	
162	  local z_tags=()
163	  local z_tag=""
164	  while IFS= read -r z_tag || test -n "${z_tag}"; do
165	    test -n "${z_tag}" || continue
166	    z_tags+=("${z_tag}")
167	  done < "${z_tags_file}"
168	
169	  echo ""
170	  printf "  Member tags (%s):\n" "${#z_tags[@]}"
171	  for z_tag in "${z_tags[@]}"; do
172	    printf "    %s\n" "${z_tag}"
173	  done

c6bcb5223 jjb:1015-6aaad8e86:₢BHAAY:W: Retired the dead enshrine-creation operation. Deleted RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc and the rbtgo_ark_enshrine quoin (mapping attribute, RBS0 operation section + include, acronym entry). Repointed the live cross-refs off the dead quoin: conjure's 404 directive and RBSFH's supply-chain-anchor chain to the bole ensconce quoin (rbtgo_lode_ensconce); RBSIR's omitted-domain note to plain legacy-enshrinement prose; RBSHR's roadmap pointer to RBSLE. Reworded the vessel-regime ANCHOR consumption prose (RBS0 rbrv_image_origin/rbrv_image_anchor + RBSRV) off the dead enshrine to-enshrines-namespace flow onto the bole-Lode capture + derived-pull-election model, matching the landed rbfd code comment — spec now slightly leads code (the populator is unwired). Zero hits on the three retired patterns repo-wide; all referenced quoins defined; fast green 151/0. Plan-gap survey surfaced that the bole derived-pull ANCHOR populator (code comments call it 'a later election') was owned by no pace; per operator decision it was folded into the reliquary inscribe-cutover, which also finalizes the ANCHOR prose against the wired populator.
6aaad8e86 jjb:1015-54bf8ba96:₢BHAAY:n: Retire the dead enshrine-creation operation. Delete RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc and the rbtgo_ark_enshrine quoin (mapping-section attribute, the RBS0 operation section + include, and the acronym-map entry) — the bole cutover already deleted its step scripts and the rbfd_enshrine gesture, leaving only a spec describing a path nothing runs. Repoint the live cross-references off the dead quoin: conjure's 404 directive and RBSFH's supply-chain-anchor chain now name the bole ensconce quoin (rbtgo_lode_ensconce); RBSIR's omitted-domain note becomes plain legacy-enshrinement prose; RBSHR's roadmap pointer moves to RBSLE-lode_ensconce.adoc. Reword the vessel-regime ANCHOR consumption prose (RBS0 rbrv_image_origin/rbrv_image_anchor + RBSRV) off the dead enshrine to-enshrines-namespace flow onto the bole-Lode capture + derived-pull-election model, matching the landed rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild comment. Zero hits on the three retired patterns repo-wide; every newly-referenced quoin (rbtgo_lode_ensconce, rbtga_lode, rbtga_touchmark, gar_lodes_namespace) is defined. The bole ANCHOR populator wiring and final ANCHOR-prose finalization were folded into the reliquary inscribe cutover per the plan-gap survey; this pace leaves that prose at the bole-model level. fast verification follows on the now-clean tree.
54bf8ba96 jjx: jjx_redocket
2d0bfbcca jjb:1015-035f2b40a::i: OFFICIUM 260610-1001-u286
035f2b40a jjb:1015-cdde7812c:₢BHAAN:W: Divine/augur grain split landed (code at cdde7812c) and comprehensively live-verified. augur (rbw-la) is the new read-only single-Lode inspect verb: it lists member tags and decodes the :rbi_vouch provenance envelope (kind/acquired/trust_grade/members[] with origin/digest/verification) via zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact on rbgjl02's vouch.json layer, reporting honest Pale trust-grade posture; divine is trimmed to enumerate-only. Kind name reads from each envelope's own kind field, so a new Lode kind needs no augur change. All three divine-inspect call sites repointed to augur.
cdde7812c jjb:1015-28b0aed57:₢BHAAN:n: Realize the divine/augur grain split: split single-Lode inspect out of divine into the new read-only augur verb (rbw-la), trim divine to enumerate-only, and implement the augur substance the spec mandates — decoding the :rbi_vouch provenance envelope (kind/acquired_at/acquired_by/capture_build/trust_grade/members[]) which divine's inspect branch never read. augur validates the touchmark folio (as banish requires it) and its <kind><YYMMDDHHMMSS> format, fetches the package member tags, then extracts the :rbi_vouch FROM-scratch artifact (rbgjl02's vouch.json layer at image root) via zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact, renders the decoded fields, and reports honest trust-grade posture — verified-against-published vs recorded-at-acquisition, Pale-honest, never over-claiming what the upstream permits. Kind name is read from the envelope's own kind field, so augur stays kind-agnostic (a new Lode kind needs no change here). New ZRBLD_AUGUR_PREFIX in the kindle; zipper enrolls rbw-la (param1) and drops divine's folio channel to none (enumerate-only); new read-only tabtarget tt/rbw-la.DirectorAugursLode.sh mirrors the divine trampoline (no BURD_INTERACTIVE). Repointed all three lifecycle divine-inspect call sites (lode/reliquary/wsl) to augur in rbtdrc_crucible.rs: the lode case carries the explicit envelope-decode assertions (trust grade + a member's oci-digest verification — markers that live inside vouch.json, never in a tag listing, so they prove decode rather than enumeration), and the reliquary case asserts the cohort N-member envelope decodes. rbtdgc_consts.rs (RBTDGC_AUGUR_LODE) and claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md build-regenerated from the zipper. Build green, shellcheck clean (209 files); fast suite and service-tier live GAR verification follow this clean-tree commit, the live run batched with adjacent paces at operator discretion per the paddock Execution posture.
28b0aed57 jjb:1015-4efa88c5c::i: OFFICIUM 260610-1000-0fdd
4efa88c5c jjb:1015-d49fb3fa2:₣BH:n: Five Fable review recommendations from the cloud-dispatch delete architecture review, one memo per correctable behavior: pin the delete builder by digest (Director-privileged floating builder), progress-aware convergence deadline (fixed 180s wrong-shaped for large webs), URLError tolerance in fire_delete, urllib socket timeouts, and the self-actAs propagation flap awaiting the first pristine gauntlet.
b093b0075 jjb:1015-e32fe143c:₢BHAAa:W: Image-backdoor path-polymorphic verbs landed and live-verified. Minted the type-blind raw trio on final-form bare-letter colophons: rbw-il (new rbfl_list — iterative GAR-path narrowing, envelope-independent, shows walking-dead debris by design), rbw-iw (generic wrest), rbw-iJ (generic jettison, now parsing both :tag and @sha256: version refs). Retired enshrinement variants iwe/iJe; the surviving hallmark/reliquary variants and the two waves that clear the deliberate iw/iJ terminal-exclusivity violation are recorded in memo-20260610-heat-BH-image-tabtarget-cleanup. Fixed the deferred 010ec044a theurge build break (invoke_count pub(crate) unreachable from the bin crate) via invoke_count()/set_invoke_count() accessors. Added the reliquary-lifecycle member-jettison theurge case (rbw-il + rbw-iJ prove tag-grain delete removes one member, leaves the sibling + Lode intact). Discovered rbtdgc_consts.rs + claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md are build-regenerated from the zipper; added a claude-rbk-acronyms.md note to head off future confusion. fast green (151/0). Verified live against the disposable depot across the full list/wrest/jettison permutation matrix — list at every grain, wrest by tag+digest, tag-jettison (tag-only, aliases survive), digest-jettison (version reaped), cross-package independence, honest-fail on tag-referenced digest delete — all correct, zero surprises. The new theurge case's standalone live run is batched per the execution posture.
e32fe143c jjb:1015-2e2ed2377:₢BHAAa:n: Image-backdoor path-polymorphic verbs: mint the type-blind raw trio on final-form bare verb letters — rbw-il (list: iterative GAR-path narrowing, new rbfl_list, envelope-independent), rbw-iw (wrest any ref), rbw-iJ (jettison a tag or @sha256: version; rbfl_jettison gains digest-ref parsing). Retire enshrinement variants iwe/iJe; hallmark/reliquary variants survive per the staged cleanup. Terminal exclusivity deliberately violated on iw/iJ (operator-blessed final form; memo-20260610-heat-BH-image-tabtarget-cleanup records the two waves that clear it). Fix the deferred 010ec044a theurge build break (invoke_count pub(crate) unreachable from the bin crate) via invoke_count()/set_invoke_count() accessors routed through the suite loop. Add the reliquary-lifecycle member-jettison case: rbw-il + rbw-iJ prove tag-grain delete removes one member and leaves siblings intact. rbtdgc_consts.rs + claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md are build-regenerated from the zipper; claude-rbk-acronyms.md gains a note flagging that to head off future confusion. Verified live against the disposable depot across list/wrest/jettison permutations — all correct.
2e2ed2377 jjb:1015-f7d65c2d7::i: OFFICIUM 260610-1000-u32k
f7d65c2d7 jjb:1015-37c73e0a1:₢BHAAT:W: Crane-embrace gate verified live against GAR: capture on gcrane+curl+gpg (docker/buildx evicted) proven by the three service-tier fixtures (lode/reliquary/wsl) green, with the unverified-live conclave single-platform fix holding. The same run discharged live verification for the preceding cloud-dispatch delete-architecture pace via the fixtures' banish/abjure legs. Surfaced and fixed a real bug the gate exposed: the bole base-anchor election (zrbfd_elect_base_anchor) buc_die'd on any non-bole Lode kind-brand, so a wsl underpin or reliquary conclave chained ahead of a conjure killed the ordain pre-submit (a real operator sequence; here batch-vouch's leaked-fact path). Fixed to no-op on reliquary/wsl brands, leave the base ANCHOR as-is, reserve buc_die for a brand outside the authored enum (commit 91666a97b); verified end-to-end by a clean service suite (161 passed, 0 failed) whose batch-vouch ordain exercised the new arm with a real wsl brand in previous/. Also repaired the theurge BURV invoke-dir isolation leak that fed the leak (per-fixture invoke-counter reset collided every fixture's first invoke on invoke-00000, so bud's current/->previous/ promotion bled prior facts into a non-chained invoke; suite loop now threads the counter monotonically, commit 010ec044a, compile/run-verification deferred to slated pace ₢BHAAc).
37c73e0a1 jjb:1015-010ec044a:₣BH:S: burv-invoke-dir-isolation-verify
010ec044a jjb:1015-91666a97b:₢BHAAT:n: Thread the BURV invoke counter across fixtures in the theurge suite loop so per-invoke dir names are suite-monotonic instead of resetting to invoke-00000 each fixture. The per-fixture reset made every fixture's first invoke reuse invoke-00000; bud's start-of-dispatch current/->previous/ promotion then leaked the prior fixture's chaining facts into a non-chained invoke's previous/ — the mechanism by which wsl-lifecycle's lode facts reached batch-vouch's ordain election and killed it. Only the suite loop carries the count forward; Context::new still defaults invoke_count to 0, so the rbtdti unit tests and the single-fixture/single-case paths are unchanged, and the chain_next logic (relative count-1) still resolves within-fixture. NOT yet compiled or run per operator instruction — verification deferred to a slated pace that checks the post-repair BURV dir count against the invocation count (pre-repair residue: 10 dirs / 32 invocations).
91666a97b jjb:1015-9086bca19:₢BHAAT:n: Base-anchor election no-ops on non-bole Lode brands instead of fatal-dying. zrbfd_elect_base_anchor read the chaining-fact kind-brand and buc_die'd on anything but bole, so a wsl underpin or reliquary conclave chained ahead of a conjure (a real operator sequence — and the batch-vouch fixture's leaked-fact path) killed the ordain at one-second-in, pre-submit. Now reliquary/wsl brands leave the base ANCHOR as-is (only a bole carries a base image to elect), reserving buc_die for a brand outside the authored enum; the brand is read before the Electing step so it no longer mislogs Electing-from-bole before dying. Header contract updated to match.
9086bca19 jjb:1015-7bec7776d::i: OFFICIUM 260609-1013-04nf
7bec7776d jjb:1015-d49fb3fa2:₢BHAAZ:W: Cloud-dispatch GAR package delete landed and live-proven. banish (Lode) + abjure (hallmark) now dispatch a Director-run delete-build via the build-assembly spine (caller-supplied serviceAccount; spine decoupled to rbfc-level so abjure cross-sources it from the rbfl process — the cinch-blessed narrow cross into made-side delete); Director gained serviceAccountUser-on-self.
---
memo-20260609-delete-architecture-spec-hygiene-anchor.md
memo-20260609-federation-canon.md
memo-20260610-heat-BH-fable-recommendation-actas-propagation-flap.md
memo-20260610-heat-BH-fable-recommendation-convergence-deadline-shape.md

memo-20260610-heat-BH-fable-recommendation-urllib-timeout.md
memo-20260610-heat-BH-image-tabtarget-cleanup.md

lastPrompt: Would you check on the progress of heat BH now?  I rewound you to save context, but we saved your concerns as memos.  I want to know if you detect any more given the fact that opus has wrapped several…

28	rbfl_jettison() {
29	  zrbfl_sentinel
30	
31	  local z_locator="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
32	
33	  # Documentation block
34	  buc_doc_brief "Jettison an image tag or version from the registry by raw ref (type-blind)"
35	  buc_doc_param "ref" "Image ref: package:tag or package@sha256:<hex>. Type-blind over any rbi_* namespace. Below-package grain only — whole-package delete is banish/abjure."
36	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
37	
38	  # Validate ref parameter
39	  test -n "${z_locator}" || buc_die "Image ref required (package:tag or package@sha256:<hex>)"
40	
41	  # Parse the image ref into package path and manifest reference. GAR's two
42	  # deletable leaves are a tag (package:tag) and a version digest
43	  # (package@sha256:<hex>); Docker Registry v2 DELETE addresses both by
44	  # /manifests/<reference>. The @ form is matched first — a digest ref carries
45	  # a colon too, so a naive split-on-colon would mangle it.
46	  local z_pkg_path z_ref
47	  case "${z_locator}" in
48	    *@*) z_pkg_path="${z_locator%@*}"; z_ref="${z_locator##*@}" ;;
49	    *:*) z_pkg_path="${z_locator%:*}"; z_ref="${z_locator##*:}" ;;
50	    *)   buc_die "Invalid image ref. Expected package:tag or package@sha256:<hex>" ;;
51	  esac
52	  test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in image ref"
53	  test -n "${z_ref}"      || buc_die "Reference (tag or digest) is empty in image ref"
54	
55	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
56	
57	  # Get OAuth token using Director credentials
58	  local z_token
59	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"
60	
61	  buc_require "Will jettison: ${z_locator}" "yes"
62	
63	  buc_step "Jettisoning: ${z_locator}"
64	
65	  # Jettison by tag reference
66	  local z_status_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}status.txt"
67	  local z_response_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}response.json"
68	  local z_stderr_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}stderr.txt"
69	
70	  rbuh_request "DELETE"                                                  \
71	                    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_ref}" \
72	                    "${z_token}"                                              \
73	                    "${z_response_file}" "${z_status_file}" "${z_stderr_file}" \
74	    || buc_die "DELETE request failed — see ${z_stderr_file}"
75	
76	  local z_http_code
77	  z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
78	  test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty"
79	
80	  if test "${z_http_code}" != "202" && test "${z_http_code}" != "204"; then
81	    local z_body="empty"
82	    if test -f "${z_response_file}"; then z_body=$(<"${z_response_file}"); fi
83	    buc_warn "Response body: ${z_body}"
84	    buc_die "Jettison failed with HTTP ${z_http_code}"
85	  fi
86	
87	  buc_success "Jettisoned or nonexistent: ${z_locator}"
88	}
89	
90	rbfl_abjure() {
91	  zrbfl_sentinel
92	
93	  local z_hallmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
94	
95	  # Documentation block
96	  buc_doc_brief "Abjure a hallmark — delete all GAR packages under rbi_hm/<hallmark>/"
97	  buc_doc_param "hallmark" "Full hallmark (e.g., c260305133650-r260305160530)"
98	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
99	
100	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Hallmark parameter required"
101	
102	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
103	  local z_token
104	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"
105	
106	  # Enumerate packages under rbi_hm/<hallmark>/ via GAR REST API.
107	  # Each immediate child of the subtree is one ark (image, vouch, pouch,
108	  # about, attest, diags). Iterating discovered children rather than a
109	  # hardcoded suffix list naturally tolerates graft's missing pouch.
110	  local -r z_subtree="${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/"
111	  buc_step "Enumerating packages under ${z_subtree}"
112	
113	  local -r z_list_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages?pageSize=1000"
114	  local -r z_list_infix="rbfl_abjure_list"
115	
116	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
117	  rbuh_require_ok "List packages for abjure" "${z_list_infix}"

file.content:
rbfl_jettison() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  local z_locator="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Jettison an image tag or version from the registry by raw ref (type-blind)"
  buc_doc_param "ref" "Image ref: package:tag or package@sha256:<hex>. Type-blind over any rbi_* namespace. Below-package grain only — whole-package delete is banish/abjure."
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Validate ref parameter
  test -n "${z_locator}" || buc_die "Image ref required (package:tag or package@sha256:<hex>)"

  # Parse the image ref into package path and manifest reference. GAR's two
  # deletable leaves are a tag (package:tag) and a version digest
  # (package@sha256:<hex>); Docker Registry v2 DELETE addresses both by
  # /manifests/<reference>. The @ form is matched first — a digest ref carries
  # a colon too, so a naive split-on-colon would mangle it.
  local z_pkg_path z_ref
  case "${z_locator}" in
    *@*) z_pkg_path="${z_locator%@*}"; z_ref="${z_locator##*@}" ;;
    *:*) z_pkg_path="${z_locator%:*}"; z_ref="${z_locator##*:}" ;;
    *)   buc_die "Invalid image ref. Expected package:tag or package@sha256:<hex>" ;;
  esac
  test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in image ref"
  test -n "${z_ref}"      || buc_die "Reference (tag or digest) is empty in image ref"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"

  # Get OAuth token using Director credentials
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"

  buc_require "Will jettison: ${z_locator}" "yes"

  buc_step "Jettisoning: ${z_locator}"

  # Jettison by tag reference
  local z_status_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}status.txt"
  local z_response_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}response.json"
  local z_stderr_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}stderr.txt"

  rbuh_request "DELETE"                                                  \
                    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_ref}" \
                    "${z_token}"                                              \
                    "${z_response_file}" "${z_status_file}" "${z_stderr_file}" \
    || buc_die "DELETE request failed — see ${z_stderr_file}"

  local z_http_code
  z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
  test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty"

  if test "${z_http_code}" != "202" && test "${z_http_code}" != "204"; then
    local z_body="empty"
    if test -f "${z_response_file}"; then z_body=$(<"${z_response_file}"); fi
    buc_warn "Response body: ${z_body}"
    buc_die "Jettison failed with HTTP ${z_http_code}"
  fi

  buc_success "Jettisoned or nonexistent: ${z_locator}"
}

rbfl_abjure() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  local z_hallmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Abjure a hallmark — delete all GAR packages under rbi_hm/<hallmark>/"
  buc_doc_param "hallmark" "Full hallmark (e.g., c260305133650-r260305160530)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Hallmark parameter required"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"

  # Enumerate packages under rbi_hm/<hallmark>/ via GAR REST API.
  # Each immediate child of the subtree is one ark (image, vouch, pouch,
  # about, attest, diags). Iterating discovered children rather than a
  # hardcoded suffix list naturally tolerates graft's missing pouch.
  local -r z_subtree="${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/"
  buc_step "Enumerating packages under ${z_subtree}"

  local -r z_list_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages?pageSize=1000"
  local -r z_list_infix="rbfl_abjure_list"

  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
  rbuh_require_ok "List packages for abjure" "${z_list_infix}"

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbfd_*)

# Derived-pull base-anchor election (bole kind). If the immediately-prior
# dispatch was a bole ensconce, it handed a touchmark forward through the depth-1
# chain; resolve it to a Lode locator and persist it as the conjure base ANCHOR
# before the build loads the vessel. This is the fetched-side "the vessel pulls
# the resolved coordinate from the capture-file": capture stays pure (writes no
# vessel config) and election writes it here, durably, so repeated conjures reuse
# the persisted ANCHOR without re-ensconcing.
#
# No buf_relay: the touchmark is consumed terminally. Relaying would forward a
# stale touchmark into a later unrelated ordain (cross-vessel leakage), so the
# depth-1 adjacency — ensconce immediately precedes the first ordain — is the
# deliberate contract, not a limitation to paper over. Absence is normal (no
# fresh capture in the chain): leave any existing ANCHOR untouched. A present
# touchmark elects only when its kind-brand is bole; a non-bole touchmark (a wsl
# underpin or reliquary conclave chained ahead of this ordain) likewise leaves
# the ANCHOR as-is. A bole presence overwrites, so a re-capture repoints.
# Args: rbrv_file
zrbfd_elect_base_anchor() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_rbrv_file="${1:?Vessel regime file required}"
  test -f "${z_rbrv_file}" || buc_die "Vessel regime file not found: ${z_rbrv_file}"

  test -f "${BURD_PREVIOUS_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK}" || {
    buc_log_args "No Lode touchmark in the chain — base ANCHOR left as-is"
    return 0
  }

  local z_brand=""
  z_brand=$(buf_read_fact "${RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND}") || buc_die "Failed to read kind-brand chaining fact"

  # Only a bole carries a base image to elect. A non-bole capture (an underpin or
  # conclave run earlier in the same depth-1 chain) hands its own touchmark
  # forward but has no base ANCHOR to give — leave the ANCHOR as-is. A brand
  # outside the authored enum is corruption.
  local z_tag=""
  case "${z_brand}" in
    "${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_BOLE}")
      z_tag="${RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE}" ;;
    "${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY}"|"${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_WSL}")
      buc_log_args "Chained Lode is '${z_brand}', not a base capture — base ANCHOR left as-is"
      return 0 ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Unknown Lode kind-brand in chaining fact: '${z_brand}'" ;;
  esac

  buc_step "Electing conjure base ANCHOR from bole capture"

  local z_touchmark=""
  z_touchmark=$(buf_read_fact "${RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK}") || buc_die "Failed to read touchmark chaining fact"
  test -n "${z_touchmark}" || buc_die "Empty touchmark in chaining fact"

  local -r z_locator="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}:${z_tag}"

  # Find the single populated base ORIGIN slot (ensconce captures exactly one).
  local z_line=""
  local z_slot=""
  local z_count=0
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    case "${z_line}" in
      RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN=?*) z_slot="1"; z_count=$((z_count + 1)) ;;
      RBRV_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN=?*) z_slot="2"; z_count=$((z_count + 1)) ;;
      RBRV_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN=?*) z_slot="3"; z_count=$((z_count + 1)) ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_rbrv_file}"
  test "${z_count}" -eq 1 \
    || buc_die "Base-anchor election needs exactly one populated RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN slot, found ${z_count}"

  # Replace-or-append the chosen slot's ANCHOR line.
  local -r z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ANCHOR"
  local -r z_anchor_line="${z_anchor_var}=${z_locator}"
  local -r z_updated_file="${ZRBFD_ELECT_PREFIX}rbrv.env"
  local z_found=false
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    if [[ "${z_line}" == ${z_anchor_var}=* ]]; then
      printf '%s\n' "${z_anchor_line}"; z_found=true
    else
      printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"
    fi
  done < "${z_rbrv_file}" > "${z_updated_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to rewrite ${z_rbrv_file} for ${z_anchor_var}"
  if [[ "${z_found}" != "true" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${z_anchor_line}" >> "${z_updated_file}" || buc_die "Failed to append ${z_anchor_var}"
  fi
  cp "${z_updated_file}" "${z_rbrv_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write updated rbrv.env"

  buc_success "Elected base ANCHOR slot ${z_slot}: ${z_locator}"
  buc_info "rbrv.env base ANCHOR updated — commit it with your usual workflow to pin this base"
}

input.content:
# Fable recommendation — elect_base_anchor slot-count die is a dormant sibling of the brand-leak ordain killer

Date: 2026-06-10
Status: recommendation from the post-wrap ₣BH review (second Fable pass); not yet acted on.

## The behavior

`zrbfd_elect_base_anchor` (rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh) requires **exactly one** populated
`RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN` slot and `buc_die`s otherwise ("needs exactly one populated slot, found N").
The check runs whenever a bole touchmark is present in the depth-1 chain — before the build submits.

## Why it is the same failure family as the fixed brand bug

Commit 91666a97b fixed: a *non-bole* fact chained ahead of an ordain killed the ordain pre-submit.
The remaining shape: a *bole* fact chained ahead of an ordain of a **multi-origin vessel**
(the RBRV regime authors slots 1–3) dies the same way — pre-submit, one second in, on a real
operator sequence (ensconce vessel A's base, then ordain multi-origin vessel B before any other
dispatch ages the chain out).
Same mechanism, same cost profile as the bug ₢BHAAT diagnosed live; only the discriminator differs
(slot count instead of kind-brand).

## Dormancy

No live vessel populates `RBRV_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN`/`_3_ORIGIN` today (checked 2026-06-10: zero hits
across rbmm_moorings and the vessel regimes), so the die is unreachable until a multi-origin
nameplate exists. It will fire on the first one that ordains behind a chained bole fact.

## Recommended repair

Mirror the brand fix: when the slot count is not exactly one, log-and-leave-the-ANCHOR-as-is
(loud `buc_log_args`, not fatal) — the election simply cannot disambiguate, and absence-of-election
is already a normal outcome of this function. Reserve `buc_die` for genuinely corrupt state.
Decide whether count==0 (bole fact present, no origin slot at all) deserves the same no-op or a
warning with a different message.

# Fable recommendation — rbfl_jettison success message claims a tolerance the code refuses

Date: 2026-06-10
Status: recommendation from the post-wrap ₣BH review (second Fable pass); not yet acted on.

## The behavior

`rbfl_jettison` (rbfld_Delete.sh) ends with `buc_success "Jettisoned or nonexistent: <ref>"`,
but the code accepts only HTTP 202/204 and `buc_die`s on everything else — including the 404 a
nonexistent ref returns from the Docker v2 manifests DELETE. The message describes an idempotent
delete; the code implements a strict one. One of them is wrong.

## Recommended repair

Tolerate 404 as success — i.e., make the code match the message, not the reverse.
Idempotent deletes are the house philosophy (`rbuh_poll_until_gone`, the rbgjl06 convergence loop's
404-is-already-gone), and a cleanup-of-last-resort verb that dies on already-gone is the wrong
shape for its job. If strictness is preferred instead, change the message to "Jettisoned" and let
404 keep dying loud.

## Adjacent note for the record (no action)

The ₢BHAAa docket cinched "honest LRO handling (`rbge_lro_ok` where GAR returns an LRO)"; the
landed verb instead rides the Docker v2 manifests DELETE, where no LRO exists, trusting 202/204.
Live verification confirmed the 202 corresponds to actual deletion, and a missed delete is visible
via `rbw-il`, so this is acceptable — but it is a trust-202 cousin of the gap this heat closed for
package-grain deletes, and worth remembering if jettison ever misbehaves.

content.text:
Review done — two new findings, both memo'd and committed (`745845953`). The heat-state summary and verdict:

**₣BH progress:** 17 of 29 paces complete. Since my last review, five paces wrapped — the delete architecture (₢BHAAZ), the crane-embrace gate (₢BHAAT), the image backdoor (₢BHAAa), the augur split (₢BHAAN), and the enshrine-spec retirement (₢BHAAY) — plus a new verification pace ₢BHAAc was slated for the BURV repair. All five of my prior recommendations were faithfully preserved as memos. Notably, my "landed-but-never-compiled code is the riskiest artifact" concern was vindicated within hours: commit 010ec044a *did* break the theurge build, and ₢BHAAa had to fix it (`invoke_count` visibility).

**What I reviewed and found clean:**

- **The BURV counter threading** (the fix behind the leaked-fact bug). I went looking for a panic path — `rbtdrc_take_context()` panics on absence, and main.rs calls it unconditionally even when a fixture errors — but the engine only ever *borrows* the context (`rbtdrc_with_ctx`); main.rs is the sole taker/setter, so the context always survives `run_fixture` including its Err path. Structurally sound. ₢BHAAc's dir-count-vs-invocation-count check remains the right live verification.
- **The brand-leak ordain fix** (91666a97b): correct, and the header contract now reads coherently — no-op for absence and non-bole brands, die only outside the authored enum.
- **The augur split**: clean design — kind read from the envelope's own field so new kinds need no augur change; live-verified with decode-proving assertions (markers that live inside vouch.json, not in tag listings). No concerns.

**The two new findings (now memos):**

1. **`zrbfd_elect_base_anchor`'s exactly-one-ORIGIN die is a dormant sibling of the bug they just fixed.** The brand fix handled a *non-bole* fact leaking ahead of an ordain; the remaining shape — a *bole* fact ahead of an ordain of a multi-origin vessel — still kills the ordain pre-submit with the slot-count die. Same mechanism, same cost profile, different discriminator. It's unreachable today (no vessel populates slot 2/3 — I checked), but it fires on the first multi-origin nameplate that ordains behind a chained capture. Recommended repair mirrors the brand fix: log-and-leave-as-is, not fatal.
2. **`rbfl_jettison` says "Jettisoned or nonexistent" but dies on 404.** The message promises an idempotent delete; the code implements a strict one. I recommend tolerating 404 — idempotent deletes are the house philosophy (`rbuh_poll_until_gone`, the convergence loop's 404-is-already-gone), and a cleanup-of-last-resort verb shouldn't die on already-gone. The memo also records, for the trail, that jettison rides the v2 manifests DELETE trusting 202 — a verified-acceptable cousin of the trust-200 gap this heat closed.

**Still pending from the first review, unchanged:** the self-actAs propagation flap awaits the first pristine gauntlet (the ₢BHAAT service run used the standing depot, as predicted it would); the delete-builder digest pin and convergence-deadline shape memos await action. The remaining heat spine (podvm immure → fanout → inscribe cutover → docs → scrub) is unaffected by anything I found.

content.text:
**Notch:** already done — `jjx_record` is self-committing, so the two memos landed as `745845953` and the tree is clean. Nothing left to commit.

**The fork — my answer is option 1, `podvm-wsl` / `podvm-native`, and I'd call it firmly:**

The decider is your own Interface Contamination Discipline: the argument flows end-to-end — CLI token → brand constant → `:rbi_vouch` envelope `kind` field → augur display. Option 1 is the only one where a single canonical form survives the whole path with no translation layer. The operator types the exact string that augur will later print back at them; `grep podvm-wsl` finds the verb invocation, the constant, the envelope, and the spec in one sweep. That end-to-end identity is worth more than the keystrokes it costs.

The other two each violate a standing rule:

- **`wsl` / `native` collides with the curated premise head-on.** `rbw-lI wsl` reads as the underpin verb's territory — the exact "wsl vs podvm-wsl visibly different" confusion the vocabulary was curated to prevent. An operator six months from now seeing `wsl` in two Lode verbs' argument slots will conflate the kinds; the premise exists because that conflation is cheap to fall into and expensive to climb out of.
- **`vw` / `vn` makes operators speak the encoding.** Kind-letters are touchmark *prefix* encoding — the divine legend exists precisely to decode them into names for humans. Making the CLI argument the letter inverts that relationship: now the human-facing surface speaks the internal scheme and needs the legend to be writable, not just readable. It also couples the operator surface to the letter allocation — if a letter ever remints, the operator vocabulary breaks with it.

One supporting precedent: the folio convention elsewhere passes meaningful monikers (`tadmor`, release points), never internal codes. And the typing cost is low-stakes — immure is a rare, deliberate Director gesture, not a hot loop.

So: `rbw-lI podvm-wsl 5.6`, argument verbatim equal to the brand. Opus's other two engineering picks (2-member wsl-family default set; pinned-version fixture that fails loud on quay rotation) both look right to me — the loud failure *is* the recorded-grade truth, and the refresh mode belongs to the following pace exactly as the docket cinches.

Watching Opus do the work, I'm now wondering if we ought to create an equivalent of the allow list for python, but instead anchored on what packages are allowed to be imported?  Make a memo if you agree, but do look at some code to ground your decision.


⏺ The operator just handed me the resolution — let me verify it concretely. There's a rbgjl06-package-delete.py
  already, and CBG is described as polyglot bash/sh + python. Let me confirm the python cloud-step precedent and
  how it's wired before I answer.

⏺ Bash(echo "=== full GCB allowlist (lines 154-170) ===" && sed -n '154,170p' Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdru_cupel.rs
      && echo "" && echo "=== is python in any allowlist? ==…)
  ⎿  Error: Exit code 1
     === full GCB allowlist (lines 154-170) ===
     pub(crate) const ZRBTDRU_GCB_ALLOWED: &[&str] = &[
         "apt-get", "awk", "cat", "curl", "cut", "docker", "gcrane", "gpg",
         "grep", "head", "ls", "openssl", "sha256sum", "shasum", "skopeo",
         "tar", "tr", "wget",
     ];

     /// Bash builtins and command-position keywords that name no external command —
     /// never flagged. Keywords with control-flow semantics ([[, ]], the loop/branch
     … +11 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ tr is actually allowed (I needn't have avoided it); tail and jq are not. The key finding: python isn't in the
  bash allowlist because the checker only lints .sh files — a .py cloud step sidesteps the bash-command
  discipline entirely. That's exactly why rbgjl06-package-delete.py is python: it parses GAR JSON. Let me
  confirm its builder and wiring (my last command aborted when the python grep found nothing).

  Searching for 1 pattern… (ctrl+o to expand)

content:
▎ Design the in-pool Cloud Build step decomposition for the immure verb — capturing selected podman-machine
  ▎ disk leaves of one quay family (quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl) into one GAR package rbi_ld/<vw|vn><stamp>,
  ▎ recorded-at-acquisition trust grade.
  ▎
  ▎ Fixed constraints (do not relitigate): (a) gcrane cp <family>@<leaf-digest> is the only registry copy tool,
  ▎ runs on gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug (busybox — no jq, no curl); (b) JSON parsing of the
  ▎ upstream OCI index is done in python on gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest (python3 + stdlib urllib/json),
  ▎ per the rbgjl06-package-delete.py precedent — jq is forbidden in bash GCB steps by curated allowlist; (c)
  ▎ steps ride a data-driven spine (rblds_Spine.sh) as recipe rows script_path|builder|id|entrypoint, sharing
  ▎ /workspace; (d) leaf selection keys on the index child descriptor's platform.architecture (alt spelling
  ▎ x86_64/aarch64) + annotations.disktype, never the layer filename; (e) each selected leaf is a
  ▎ single-platform manifest so the package stays flat (atomic banish); (f) a blob-residency guard (registry-v2
  ▎ HEAD, Content-Length == declared layer size) must run against the GAR copy after the cp; (g) the existing
  ▎ rbgjl02 gcrane vouch-push is reused unchanged and reads /workspace/lode_<stamp>_vouch.json +
  ▎ /workspace/lode_stamps.txt.
  ▎
  ▎ Deliver: the ordered step list (id, builder image, entrypoint, one-line purpose), the exact /workspace files
  ▎ each step produces/consumes (the inter-step contract), which step authors the provenance envelope and
  ▎ writes the host-facing /builder/outputs/output slot the spine extracts, and any risk flags (builder egress
  ▎ to public quay, anon-token flow, python3 availability, ordering hazards). Read
  ▎ Tools/rbk/rbgjl/rbgjl06-package-delete.py, rbgjl0{1,3,4,5}*.sh, Tools/rbk/rbldw_Underpin.sh,
  ▎ Tools/rbk/rbldr_Reliquary.sh, and Tools/rbk/rblds_Spine.sh for the established shapes. Return a blueprint,
  ▎ not code.

7:# Cloud Build Guide (CBG) — Step-Body Discipline
9:## Purpose
27:## How this document is organized — two genres on purpose
44:## Core Philosophy
70:## The Cloud Step Environment
86:## Authored Disciplines (prose — internalize, don't cite)
88:### Crash-fast, spelled per language
123:### The Native-Serializer Rule
138:# No jq inside the step — values are controlled; none can carry a quote.
148:### Separate pure logic from network ops
155:### Step-body skeleton
164:### GAR authentication idiom
183:## Cited Rules (numbered — each has, or will have, a citer)
186:Families: `CBi_` cloud-step invariants (any language), `CBb_` bash/sh, `CBp_`
190:### CBi — Cloud Step Invariants
192:#### ✅ CBi_101: Substitutions are inputs — assert presence
202:#### ✅ CBi_102: The `/workspace` boundary — non-secret only, secrets step-local, guard every read
220:#### ✅ CBi_103: Every state change is idempotent under retry
229:#### ✅ CBi_104: Code to the builder image's pinned runtime
243:### CBb — Bash / sh Dialect
245:#### ✅ CBb_101: Guarded `$( … )` with `pipefail` is permitted in-step — BCG's temp-file mandate is relaxed
266:#### ✅ CBb_102: Capture substitutions into shell vars before snippets read them
280:### CBp — Python Dialect
282:#### ❌ CBp_101: The python helper preamble is duplicated — a known reuse gap
297:### CBh — Host-Composition Seam (what a body is authored against)
302:#### ✅ CBh_101: Shared bash logic is spliced on the host via `#@rbgjs_include`
330:Bash-only today — see CBp_101 for the python gap. The recipe row's `entrypoint`
337:#### ✅ CBh_102: The substitution blob is opaque to the spine; coverage is validated at dispatch
350:#### ✅ CBh_103: A step returns data to the host via `buildStepOutputs`

content:
That chat has a question from you.  Would you prepare a response I can paste to him?


▎ Design the in-pool Cloud Build step decomposition for the immure verb — capturing selected podman-machine
  ▎ disk leaves of one quay family (quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl) into one GAR package rbi_ld/<vw|vn><stamp>,
  ▎ recorded-at-acquisition trust grade.
  ▎
  ▎ Fixed constraints (do not relitigate): (a) gcrane cp <family>@<leaf-digest> is the only registry copy tool,
  ▎ runs on gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug (busybox — no jq, no curl); (b) JSON parsing of the
  ▎ upstream OCI index is done in python on gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest (python3 + stdlib urllib/json),
  ▎ per the rbgjl06-package-delete.py precedent — jq is forbidden in bash GCB steps by curated allowlist; (c)
  ▎ steps ride a data-driven spine (rblds_Spine.sh) as recipe rows script_path|builder|id|entrypoint, sharing
  ▎ /workspace; (d) leaf selection keys on the index child descriptor's platform.architecture (alt spelling
  ▎ x86_64/aarch64) + annotations.disktype, never the layer filename; (e) each selected leaf is a
  ▎ single-platform manifest so the package stays flat (atomic banish); (f) a blob-residency guard (registry-v2
  ▎ HEAD, Content-Length == declared layer size) must run against the GAR copy after the cp; (g) the existing
  ▎ rbgjl02 gcrane vouch-push is reused unchanged and reads /workspace/lode_<stamp>_vouch.json +
  ▎ /workspace/lode_stamps.txt.
  ▎
  ▎ Deliver: the ordered step list (id, builder image, entrypoint, one-line purpose), the exact /workspace files
  ▎ each step produces/consumes (the inter-step contract), which step authors the provenance envelope and
  ▎ writes the host-facing /builder/outputs/output slot the spine extracts, and any risk flags (builder egress
  ▎ to public quay, anon-token flow, python3 availability, ordering hazards). Read
  ▎ Tools/rbk/rbgjl/rbgjl06-package-delete.py, rbgjl0{1,3,4,5}*.sh, Tools/rbk/rbldw_Underpin.sh,
  ▎ Tools/rbk/rbldr_Reliquary.sh, and Tools/rbk/rblds_Spine.sh for the established shapes. Return a blueprint,
  ▎ not code.

input.description: See subprocess invocations in verify-provenance step

content.content:
for digest, arch, variant in entries:
        ps = f"{arch}{variant}"
        fr = f"{attest_full_ref}@{digest}"
        print(f"  {ps}: fetching provenance...")

        result = subprocess.run(
            ["gcloud", "artifacts", "docker", "images", "describe",
             fr, "--format", "json", "--show-provenance"],
            capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
        )
        prov_data = json.loads(result.stdout)

        with open(f"/workspace/prov-{ps}.json", "w") as f:
            f.write(result.stdout)

        # Extract v1.0 DSSE envelope
        envelope = None
        for p in prov_data.get("provenance_summary", {}).get("provenance", []):
            if "intoto_slsa_v1" in p.get("noteName", ""):
                envelope = p.get("envelope")
                break
        if not envelope:
            die(f"No v1.0 envelope for {ps}")

        with open(f"/workspace/env-{ps}.json", "w") as f:
            json.dump(envelope, f, indent=2)
            f.write("\n")

        # Verify keyid
        ak = envelope["signatures"][0]["keyid"]
        if ak != expected_keyid:
            die(f"keyid mismatch: {ak}")

        # Decode payload and signature (base64url)
        payload_raw = b64url_decode(envelope["payload"])
        sig_raw     = b64url_decode(envelope["signatures"][0]["sig"])

        pl_file  = f"/workspace/pl-{ps}.raw"
        sig_file = f"/workspace/sig-{ps}.bin"
        with open(pl_file, "wb") as f:
            f.write(payload_raw)
        with open(sig_file, "wb") as f:
            f.write(sig_raw)

        # Construct PAE (Pre-Authentication Encoding)
        payload_type = "application/vnd.in-toto+json"
        pae_header = f"DSSEv1 {len(payload_type)} {payload_type} {len(payload_raw)} ".encode()
        pae_file = f"/workspace/pae-{ps}.bin"
        with open(pae_file, "wb") as f:
            f.write(pae_header + payload_raw)

        # Verify with openssl
        verify_result = subprocess.run(
            ["openssl", "dgst", "-sha256",
             "-verify", "/workspace/keys/google-hosted-worker.pub",
             "-signature", sig_file, pae_file],
            capture_output=True, text=True,
        )
        if verify_result.returncode != 0:
            die(f"DSSE verify FAILED for {ps}")

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 03: Conclave the build-tool cohort into a Lode (capture) via gcrane
3	# Builder: gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug (Google-hosted, always pullable —
4	#          conclave captures the reliquary tools themselves, so it cannot bootstrap
5	#          from a reliquary; gcrane authenticates GAR ambiently via its google.Keychain
6	#          -> ADC -> the GCE metadata server as the Mason SA, no explicit login. The
7	#          :debug variant carries /busybox/sh for the orchestration. Auth canon: RBSCB)
8	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
9	#                _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE, _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA,
10	#                _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY
11	#
12	# Pull each build-tool image from upstream, tag it into ONE GAR package
13	# rbi_ld/<stamp> under the clean member tag :rbi_<tool>, and push. Author the
14	# batch provenance envelope (members[] one per tool — the cardinality axis) and
15	# stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
16	# /builder/outputs/output. Single-platform (linux/amd64) — tool images run as GCB
17	# steps on amd64 workers; gcrane cp --platform linux/amd64 copies just the amd64
18	# manifest registry->registry (daemonless — no docker daemon, no pull/tag/push).
19	#
20	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>     (one package = one Lode)
21	# Member tags on that package, each a distinct tool manifest:
22	#   :<TAG_SPRUE><tool>   e.g. rbi_gcloud, rbi_skopeo   (clean scheme — no digest layer)
23	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
24	
25	set -euo pipefail
26	echo "=== Conclave build-tool cohort into a Lode ==="
27	
28	STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
29	test -n "${STAMP}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing" >&2; exit 1; }
30	
31	PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
32	echo "Lode package: ${PKG}"
33	
34	# Tool image cohort (short-name|upstream-ref) — authoritative co-versioned set for
35	# GCB step execution; mirrors the legacy inscribe manifest (rbgji01) verbatim (the
36	# two coexist during the reliquary cutover). gcloud, docker, and gcrane are
37	# Google-hosted (gcr.io); the rest are third-party. gcrane rides the :debug variant
38	# so the resolved cohort member keeps the /busybox/sh shell its capture steps need.
39	# The cohort is the heredoc feeding the while-read loop at its `done`, below — this
40	# step runs under /busybox/sh (gcrane:debug's only shell), which has no bash arrays,
41	# so the cohort cannot be expressed as the `MANIFEST=( … )` array rbgji01 (running
42	# under bash) still uses; a while-read over a `|`-split heredoc is the POSIX form,
43	# matching rbgjl02's stamp loop.
44	
45	# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole cohort.
46	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
47	
48	# Accumulate the envelope members[] as we capture (one element per tool). No jq
49	# dependency — values are controlled (tool name, upstream ref, hex digest, SA
50	# email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
51	MEMBERS=''
52	MFIRST=true
53	
54	while IFS='|' read -r NAME UPSTREAM; do
55	  test -n "${NAME}" || continue
56	  MEMBER_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_SPRUE}${NAME}"
57	  DEST="${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}"
58	
59	  echo "--- ${NAME}: ${UPSTREAM} -> ${DEST} ---"
60	
61	  # gcrane cp copies registry->registry by digest, daemonless (no docker pull/tag/push).
62	  # --platform linux/amd64 flattens a multi-arch upstream to the SINGLE amd64 manifest.
63	  # This is conclave-ONLY and must not leak to other Lode kinds (bole/wsl keep full-

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 03: Conclave the build-tool cohort into a Lode (capture) via gcrane
# Builder: gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug (Google-hosted, always pullable —
#          conclave captures the reliquary tools themselves, so it cannot bootstrap
#          from a reliquary; gcrane authenticates GAR ambiently via its google.Keychain
#          -> ADC -> the GCE metadata server as the Mason SA, no explicit login. The
#          :debug variant carries /busybox/sh for the orchestration. Auth canon: RBSCB)
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
#                _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE, _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA,
#                _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY
#
# Pull each build-tool image from upstream, tag it into ONE GAR package
# rbi_ld/<stamp> under the clean member tag :rbi_<tool>, and push. Author the
# batch provenance envelope (members[] one per tool — the cardinality axis) and
# stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
# /builder/outputs/output. Single-platform (linux/amd64) — tool images run as GCB
# steps on amd64 workers; gcrane cp --platform linux/amd64 copies just the amd64
# manifest registry->registry (daemonless — no docker daemon, no pull/tag/push).
#
# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>     (one package = one Lode)
# Member tags on that package, each a distinct tool manifest:
#   :<TAG_SPRUE><tool>   e.g. rbi_gcloud, rbi_skopeo   (clean scheme — no digest layer)
# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Conclave build-tool cohort into a Lode ==="

STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
test -n "${STAMP}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing" >&2; exit 1; }

PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
echo "Lode package: ${PKG}"

# Tool image cohort (short-name|upstream-ref) — authoritative co-versioned set for
# GCB step execution; mirrors the legacy inscribe manifest (rbgji01) verbatim (the
# two coexist during the reliquary cutover). gcloud, docker, and gcrane are
# Google-hosted (gcr.io); the rest are third-party. gcrane rides the :debug variant
# so the resolved cohort member keeps the /busybox/sh shell its capture steps need.
# The cohort is the heredoc feeding the while-read loop at its `done`, below — this
# step runs under /busybox/sh (gcrane:debug's only shell), which has no bash arrays,
# so the cohort cannot be expressed as the `MANIFEST=( … )` array rbgji01 (running
# under bash) still uses; a while-read over a `|`-split heredoc is the POSIX form,
# matching rbgjl02's stamp loop.

# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole cohort.
ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

# Accumulate the envelope members[] as we capture (one element per tool). No jq
# dependency — values are controlled (tool name, upstream ref, hex digest, SA
# email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
MEMBERS=''
MFIRST=true

while IFS='|' read -r NAME UPSTREAM; do
  test -n "${NAME}" || continue
  MEMBER_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_SPRUE}${NAME}"
  DEST="${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}"

  echo "--- ${NAME}: ${UPSTREAM} -> ${DEST} ---"

  # gcrane cp copies registry->registry by digest, daemonless (no docker pull/tag/push).
  # --platform linux/amd64 flattens a multi-arch upstream to the SINGLE amd64 manifest.
  # This is conclave-ONLY and must not leak to other Lode kinds (bole/wsl keep full-
  # fidelity capture): the reliquary cohort is consumed solely as GCB step images on
  # amd64 workers, so the other platforms are dead bytes — and a full multi-arch index
  # makes the Lode package a parent-index/child-manifest web that GAR's `packages
  # delete` cannot unwind in one cascade (it removes a parent, GAR auto-deletes the
  # protected children, then the cascade reaches an already-gone child and fails
  # NOT_FOUND). Phenomenon + references: RBSLC (conclave) and RBSLB (banish).
  gcrane --platform linux/amd64 cp "${UPSTREAM}" "${DEST}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane cp failed for ${UPSTREAM} -> ${DEST}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Record the upstream manifest-list digest for the envelope. gcrane digest (no
  # --platform) streams the tag's stored index digest (sha256:...) — the same canonical
  # value docker's RepoDigests reported, unchanged by the single-platform copy above, so
  # recorded digests stay identical to the pre-eviction docker path. CBb_101 applies.
  DIGEST=$(gcrane digest "${UPSTREAM}") \
    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane digest failed for ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }
  test -n "${DIGEST}" || { echo "FATAL: empty digest for ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }

  echo "${NAME} captured: ${DEST} (${DIGEST})"

  if [ "${MFIRST}" = "true" ]; then MFIRST=false; else MEMBERS="${MEMBERS},"; fi
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}{"
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"name\":\"${MEMBER_TAG}\","
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"origin\":\"${UPSTREAM}\","
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"digest\":\"${DIGEST}\","
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"verification\":\"oci-digest\","
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"tags\":[\"${MEMBER_TAG}\"]"
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}}"
done <<'MANIFEST'
gcloud|gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest
docker|gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker:latest
alpine|docker.io/library/alpine:latest
syft|docker.io/anchore/syft:latest
binfmt|docker.io/tonistiigi/binfmt:latest
skopeo|quay.io/skopeo/stable:latest
gcrane|gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug
MANIFEST

# Author the batch provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and
# the host capture-file). members[] is the cardinality axis — N for the reliquary
# cohort, where bole carries 1.
ENVELOPE='{'
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"reliquary\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[${MEMBERS}]}"

# Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact). The
# stamps file is the step-02 contract; conclave produces exactly one Lode.
printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

# Host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope). One slot —
# conclave produces exactly one Lode (the cohort is one package).
RESULT="{\"slot_1\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}}"

echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
echo "${RESULT}"

# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts the touchmark -> capture-file).
mkdir -p /builder/outputs
printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output

echo "=== Conclave capture step complete ==="

content.content: 89:... *Single-platform capture is conclave-only and load-bearing.* The cohort is consumed solely as GCB step images on amd64 workers, so other platforms are dead bytes — and capturing the full multi-arch index would make the Lode package a parent-index / child-manifest web that GAR `packages delete` cannot unwind in one cascade: it removes a parent, GAR auto-deletes the child manifests it had protected, then the cascade reaches an already-gone child and fails `NOT_FOUND` (code 5). bole and wsl keep full-fidelity capture; the host-side banish carries that latent delete risk until the delete path is reassessed (see link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/repositories/cleanup-policy[GAR cleanup policy] — child images referenced by a parent manifest are not deletable until the parent is removed). This `--platform` flag must not propagate to other Lode kinds.
