# Fable recommendation — python cloud steps need an import allowlist (and a subprocess policy)

Date: 2026-06-10
Status: recommendation, grounded in code (second Fable review pass); not yet acted on. Operator-prompted:
"should we create an equivalent of the bash command allowlist for python, anchored on imports?"

## Verdict: yes — and the gap is already carrying live traffic

The cupel's supply-chain conformance walks `*.sh` only (`zrbtdru_walk_sh`,
`ZRBTDRU_UNIVERSE_FILE_EXT` in rbtdru_cupel.rs). Four python cloud steps exist today —
`rbgja01-discover-platforms.py`, `rbgja03-build-info-per-platform.py`,
`rbgjl06-package-delete.py`, `rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py` — and sit entirely outside the
conformance surface. A `.py` step sidesteps the bash-command discipline by construction.

The live specimen proving the hole is not hypothetical: **rbgjv02 `subprocess.run`s `gcloud`** —
a command absent from `ZRBTDRU_GCB_ALLOWED` — invisibly to the cupel. The invocation itself may be
legitimate (the SLSA provenance `describe`), but it is *unreviewed by the mechanism*, which is the
whole point of having one. CBG already names the rule this would mechanize (CBi_104 "code to the
builder image's pinned runtime") and already has the `CBp_` python family with a known-gap marker
(CBp_101) — the import allowlist is the missing citer.

## Recommended shape

Extend the cupel with a `*.py` walk over the step directories and two checks:

1. **Import allowlist, anchored on the module root** of every `import X` / `from X import Y`.
   Current empirical floor (the union of all four steps): `base64, datetime, io, json, os, sys,
   tarfile, time, urllib`. Everything else flagged. `importlib`, `__import__`, `exec`, `eval`
   banned outright — dynamic import defeats static conformance. Third-party imports (`requests`
   et al.) are the real quarry: they bind a step to the floating builder's unpinned pip set, the
   exact drift class the bash allowlist exists to stop. Stdlib-only is the right floor.

2. **Subprocess policy — the bridge between the two universes.** `subprocess` lets a python step
   shell out past both disciplines. Either ban it in steps (python steps exist for in-process
   JSON/HTTP work; shelling out belongs in bash steps), or — since rbgjv02 uses it today — scan
   `subprocess.run([...])` argv[0] literals against the same `ZRBTDRU_GCB_ALLOWED` list: one tool
   floor, two languages. Under that option, `gcloud` needs explicit adjudication (add it to the
   allowlist, or convert rbgjv02's describe call to REST urllib like every other step).

## Interaction with the standing builder-pin memo

memo-20260610-heat-BH-fable-recommendation-pin-delete-builder flags the floating builder under a
privileged identity; this memo is its step-body complement. Floating builder × unscanned imports
compound: the builder decides what `import requests` resolves to. Pinning bounds the supply;
the allowlist bounds the demand.

85	. *Capture Step (in-pool — rbgjl03)*:
86	.. For each tool in the co-versioned cohort manifest (`<short-name>|<upstream-ref>`):
87	... Copy the upstream image registry->registry with `gcrane --platform linux/amd64 cp` (daemonless — no docker pull/tag/push; the single amd64 manifest only) into the one Lode package under the clean member tag `:rbi_<tool>` — a distinct manifest per tool, all sharing the one package; no digest or fingerprint layer
88	... Record the upstream tag's manifest-list digest with `gcrane digest` for the envelope — the same canonical value docker's `RepoDigests` reported, so recorded digests are unchanged from the pre-eviction docker path
89	... *Single-platform capture is conclave-only and load-bearing.* The cohort is consumed solely as GCB step images on amd64 workers, so other platforms are dead bytes — and capturing the full multi-arch index would make the Lode package a parent-index / child-manifest web that GAR `packages delete` cannot unwind in one cascade: it removes a parent, GAR auto-deletes the child manifests it had protected, then the cascade reaches an already-gone child and fails `NOT_FOUND` (code 5). bole and wsl keep full-fidelity capture; the host-side banish carries that latent delete risk until the delete path is reassessed (see link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/repositories/cleanup-policy[GAR cleanup policy] — child images referenced by a parent manifest are not deletable until the parent is removed). This `--platform` flag must not propagate to other Lode kinds.
90	.. Author the
91	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
92	envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-1`, trust grade `verified-against-published`, `members[]` one element per tool — the cardinality axis) and stage it on the {rbtgr_workspace}

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 09: Blob-residency guard for the captured podvm leaves (anti-hollow-mirror)
# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker (Google-hosted, Debian — carries curl; the
#          gcrane:debug capture builder has no curl, so the registry-v2 HEAD guard
#          lives here, after the cp). The Mason SA token comes from the metadata
#          server (rbgjs token-fetch).
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP
#
# Note: this script runs inside a Cloud Build container, not under BCG module
# discipline (CBG governs).
#
# Step 08 gcrane-cp'd each selected leaf into rbi_ld/<stamp> and confirmed the
# MANIFEST digest is faithful. This step confirms the BLOB BYTES actually reside: a
# GAR registry-v2 blob HEAD returns Content-Length; assert it equals the leaf
# manifest's declared layer size (staged by rbgjl07 in the selection list). This is
# the anti-hollow-mirror guard the recorded trust grade demands — a registry can
# hold a manifest whose large layer never fully landed; the digest check alone would
# not catch it (memo-20260608 §1, proven against 1.13 GB / 245 MB / 200 MB / 194 MB
# blobs). curl HEAD, no image tool, no blob download.

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 07: Select podvm disk leaves from a quay family index (the immure select)
# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker (Google-hosted, always pullable; Debian-based
#          — carries curl, and jq is apt-installed below if absent). This step does
#          the JSON-bearing work the gcrane:debug capture step (rbgjl08, busybox: no
#          jq, no curl) cannot: it anon-reads the PUBLIC quay family index over the
#          registry-v2 API, selects the curated {disktype × arch} leaves by their
#          index child DESCRIPTOR (platform.architecture + annotations.disktype, never
#          the layer filename — unreliable per memo-20260608 §3.4), authors the
#          recorded-grade provenance envelope, and stages a selection list for the
#          capture + residency + vouch steps that follow. Mirrors underpin's split
#          (rbgjl04 fetch/verify on Debian -> rbgjl05 wrap on gcrane): the JSON tool
#          (jq) and the registry tool (gcrane) live in disjoint builder images.
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
#                _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE, _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA,
#                _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY, _RBGL_PODVM_BRAND, _RBGL_PODVM_FAMILY,
#                _RBGL_PODVM_VERSION, _RBGL_PODVM_SELECTION
#
# Note: this script runs inside a Cloud Build container, not under BCG module
# discipline (CBG governs).
#
# podvm is recorded-at-acquisition: quay rotates these images out within days and
# publishes no durable checksum, so RB attests only the leaf digest observed at
# capture. There is no checksum to verify (unlike wsl); the integrity guard is the
# downstream blob-residency HEAD (rbgjl09) plus gcrane cp's digest-faithful copy.
#
# Selection list staged at /workspace/immure_selection.txt — one row per selected
# leaf, the contract for rbgjl08 (capture) and rbgjl09 (residency):
#   <member_tag>|<leaf_manifest_digest>|<layer_blob_digest>|<layer_blob_size>
# The envelope (/workspace/lode_<stamp>_vouch.json) and the stamp roster
# (/workspace/lode_stamps.txt) are the contract for rbgjl02 (vouch push).

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Select podvm disk leaves from quay family index ==="

STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
FAMILY="${_RBGL_PODVM_FAMILY}"
VERSION="${_RBGL_PODVM_VERSION}"
SELECTION="${_RBGL_PODVM_SELECTION}"
test -n "${STAMP}"     || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing"     >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${FAMILY}"    || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_PODVM_FAMILY missing"   >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${VERSION}"   || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_PODVM_VERSION missing"  >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${SELECTION}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_PODVM_SELECTION missing">&2; exit 1; }

# jq is the JSON contract of this step; ensure it on the Debian builder (apt path
(eval):1: == not found

The in-flight draft surfaces the real issue: **rbgjl07 as drafted is bash + jq on the docker builder, with jq apt-installed at build time** — which contradicts the question's own fixed constraint (b), would fail the cupel (jq isn't in `ZRBTDRU_GCB_ALLOWED`), and apt-installs an unpinned binary into a privileged build. My blueprint answer addresses that head-on. Here's the paste-ready response:

---

**Blueprint: immure in-pool step decomposition** (from Fable, grounded in rbgjl01–06, rbldw/rbldr, the spine, and your in-flight 07/08/09 drafts)

**Ordered step list (one build, four steps, spine recipe rows):**

| # | id | builder | entrypoint | purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | `immure-select` (rbgjl07) | `gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest` | **python3** | Anon-read the quay family index over registry-v2; select curated leaves by descriptor `platform.architecture` + `annotations.disktype`; author the recorded-grade envelope; stage the selection list |
| 1 | `immure-capture` (rbgjl08) | `gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug` | busybox | Per selection row: `gcrane cp <family>@<leaf-digest> <pkg>:<member-tag>` — digest-addressed, single-platform leaf, daemonless; GAR auth ambient (google.Keychain → metadata → Mason) |
| 2 | `immure-residency` (rbgjl09) | `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker` | bash | Anti-hollow-mirror guard: registry-v2 blob HEAD against the GAR copy, `Content-Length == declared layer size` per selection row (curl is allowlisted; token via rbgjs token-fetch) |
| 3 | `assemble-push-vouch` (rbgjl02) | `gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug` | busybox | **Unchanged** — reads stamps + vouch.json, pushes the `:rbi_vouch` FROM-scratch artifact |

**The one correction to the in-flight draft, and it's firm: rewrite rbgjl07 as python3 on gcloud:latest, not bash+jq on the docker builder.** The current draft apt-installs jq if absent — that's three violations in one: (a) `jq` is not in `ZRBTDRU_GCB_ALLOWED`, so the step fails the cupel the moment the conformance walk covers it; (b) apt-installing at build time pulls an unpinned binary from Debian repos into a privileged build — exactly the supply-chain drift the allowlist regime exists to prevent; (c) it contradicts the fixed constraint you stated. Python needs none of it: urllib does the quay anon-token dance, `json` replaces jq, `json.dumps` authors the envelope (the native-serializer rule, better honored in python than the bash string-building conclave was forced into). rbgjl06 and rbgja01 are the precedents; python3+stdlib is known-present on gcloud:latest. The 07 header's *logic* (descriptor-keyed selection, selection-list contract, recorded-grade rationale) is right — keep all of it, port the body.

**/workspace inter-step contract:**

- `/workspace/immure_selection.txt` — produced by 07; consumed by 08 and 09. Row: `<member_tag>|<leaf_manifest_digest>|<layer_blob_digest>|<layer_blob_size>` (busybox `IFS='|' read` splittable; no field can carry `|`).
- `/workspace/lode_<stamp>_vouch.json` + `/workspace/lode_stamps.txt` — produced by 07; consumed by rbgjl02. Envelope `kind` = the brand token verbatim (`podvm-wsl`), members[] one per leaf: `{name: member_tag, origin: <family>@<leaf_digest>, digest, verification, tags:[member_tag]}`.
- `/builder/outputs/output` — written by **07** (it owns the envelope), shape `{"slot_1":{"stamp":"<stamp>","vouch":<envelope>}}` matching conclave/underpin. ⚠️ The spine extracts by **step index** — rbldv's `zrbld_spine_extract` slot must be **0** (select is the first step), not the last-step index. Pin this with a comment at the extract call.

Envelope-at-select is sound for recorded-grade: the digests come from the index descriptors (the recorded truth), gcrane cp is digest-faithful, and the envelope only ships if steps 1–2 pass, because the vouch push runs last. That ordering — **rbgjl02 strictly after rbgjl09** — is itself part of the contract: never let the vouch artifact precede the residency guard.

**Risk flags:**

1. **Quay anon-token flow**: the first GET 401s with `WWW-Authenticate`; fetch `https://quay.io/v2/auth?service=quay.io&scope=repository:podman/machine-os-wsl:pull` for an anonymous token, retry with Bearer. Anonymous rate-limiting is the failure mode — fail loud, don't retry-loop.
2. **Egress posture**: step 0 (urllib) and step 1 (gcrane) reach quay.io *plus its blob-CDN redirect hosts*. TETHER passes upstream registries already (bole pulls docker.io), but quay's redirect targets are a distinct hostname set — if the worker-pool egress posture is ever tightened, this is the first breakage point. Worth one line in `rbw-di`'s check inventory.
3. **Alt-spelling selection is literal**: this family's descriptors say `x86_64`/`aarch64`, not `amd64`/`arm64`. Match the curated set literally, never normalize — and when a curated entry matches nothing, die listing every available `(architecture, disktype)` pair, so quay rotation reads as a clear inventory diff, not a mystery.
4. **Assert exactly one layer per leaf** in 07. The selection-row format encodes one blob per leaf; if quay ever ships a multi-layer disk leaf, a silent `layers[0]` would residency-check one blob and ignore the rest. Die loud on `len(layers) != 1`.
5. **buildStepOutputs ceiling**: the slot is size-capped (~4KB). Two wsl-family members fit easily; the native family's 8-leaf curation next pace approaches the ceiling with long origin refs — check before reusing the envelope-in-slot shape there.
6. **Idempotency under GCB retry** (CBi_103): all four steps are naturally idempotent (digest-addressed cp, HEAD reads, envelope rewrite) — keep it that way; no append-mode writes to the selection list (07 must truncate-then-write).
7. **Floating builders**: gcloud:latest and the docker builder are unpinned (the standing bootstrap-pin itch). Bounded here — capture runs as Mason, writer-only — but note immure adds two more floating-builder rides to that itch's inventory.

---

Also done this turn, committed as `0addd3cc8` against ₣BH: the **python-import-allowlist memo** (verdict: yes — the cupel walks `*.sh` only, four python steps sit outside conformance, and rbgjv02 already subprocess-runs `gcloud`, a command absent from the allowlist; recommended import floor + subprocess policy are in the memo) — your immure question and that memo turned out to be the same finding wearing two hats, since 07-as-python lands cleanly inside the proposed import discipline. Plus two more disproven-premise residue sites my first sweep missed (rbgjl03's single-platform comment and RBSLC's matching passage, both still telling the NOT_FOUND-cascade story) — fixed to the proven FAILED_PRECONDITION mechanism. Opus's in-flight `rbgjl09` modification was left untouched per multi-officium discipline.

1	<!--
2	Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
3	SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
4	See LICENSE for terms.
5	-->
6	
7	# Cloud Build Guide (CBG) — Step-Body Discipline
8	
9	## Purpose
10	
11	CBG codifies the discipline for authoring the **body of a single Google Cloud
12	Build step** — the script that runs inside one step's builder container. It is a
13	foreign-environment sibling to BCG (host bash) and RCG (host Rust): it shares
14	their philosophy — crash-fast, no silent failures, load-bearing complexity — but
15	diverges entirely on mechanics, because a step body executes in an environment
16	with none of the host kit's scaffolding.
17	
18	CBG governs **single-step precision**: what goes inside one step's script. The
19	one seam it also covers is **how a step body is composed and dispatched** (the
20	include splice, the substitution contract, the return channel), because a body
21	must be authored *against* that machinery.
22	
23	CBG is **polyglot**. A step body is bash, POSIX `sh`, or `python3` — the recipe
24	selects the interpreter per step. The cloud-step disciplines are
25	language-neutral; each language honors them with its own idiom.
26	
27	## How this document is organized — two genres on purpose
28	
29	CBG deliberately separates two kinds of knowledge, because they earn their form
30	differently:
31	
32	- **Authored Disciplines** (prose, no IDs) — systematic, interderivable patterns
33	  for code *we* write. Like BCG, these are internalized, not cited by number;
34	  numbering them would be ceremony with no citers.
35	- **Cited Rules** (numbered `CB*-`) — discrete facts about the cloud Palisade we do
36	  *not* control (surprises, divergences, a recorded gap), each of which
37	  something will *point at*: a step comment justifying a workaround, a memo
38	  recording a probe, a review flagging a violation, or the dispatch validator.
39	  An ID earns its existence only when a citer will exist.
40	
41	This split is itself an instance of the load-bearing test (BCG Core Philosophy):
42	catalog what is foreign; systematize what is yours.
43	
44	## Core Philosophy
45	
46	**The step body has no kit runtime.** On the host, BCG code leans on `buc_die`,
47	`buc_step`, the kindle/sentinel lifecycle, `buv_*` validation, and the module/CLI
48	gateway. **None of that exists inside a step.** A step body is a single file
49	shipped into a vendor builder container and run with a bare interpreter. Every
50	guarantee BCG gets from BUK, a step body must re-establish in a few lines of raw
51	bash or stdlib python. The philosophy survives the crossing; the mechanics are
52	rebuilt minimally, in-language. This is the relationship WSG has to BCG: the same
53	discipline, re-expressed for an environment BCG's helpers cannot reach.
54	
55	**Crash-fast is non-negotiable, and louder here.** A step's stdout/stderr *is*
56	the Cloud Build log — the only forensic surface. There is no transcript file, no
57	`buc_log_*` sink, no temp directory to inspect afterward. A failure that does not
58	print and exit non-zero is a failure that vanishes. Every fallible operation dies
59	loudly, at its location, with a message naming what failed.
60	
61	**The vendor is at the Palisade.** Cloud Build, the metadata server, the builder
62	images, and the substitution machinery are not ours to edit (Rules of
63	Engagement). We characterize their behavior precisely, contain our dependence at
64	named seams, and never paper over it. The Cited Rules below *are* that
65	characterization.
66	
67	**Load-bearing complexity still rules.** Reuse only the irreducibly identical
68	core; leave kind-specific assembly in the step (RBSCJ composition rationale).
69	
70	## The Cloud Step Environment
71	
72	A step body runs under conditions a host script never faces. Each is a fault
73	domain — the source of most Cited Rules below.
74	
75	| Condition | Consequence for the body |
76	|-----------|--------------------------|
77	| **Substitutions arrive as environment variables** (`_RBGx_*`, expanded at submit via `automapSubstitutions`). | Read from the environment, not argv; they may be empty (CBi_101). |
78	| **`/workspace` is a shared, ephemeral mount** seen by every step, destroyed at build end. | The only inter-step channel; non-secret by invariant; guard every read (CBi_102). |
79	| **Steps can be retried silently** on transient failure. | Every state change must be idempotent (CBi_103). |
80	| **Authentication is ambient**, via the metadata server. | Tokens are fetched in-memory, used, discarded — never to `/workspace` (CBi_102). |
81	| **The interpreter is the builder image's, pinned** (e.g. `gcloud` ships python 3.10). | Code to the shipped runtime; guard newer features (CBi_104). |
82	| **No native step reuse** (no anchors, no includes). | Shared bash logic is composed in on the host before submit (CBh_101). |
83	
84	---
85	
86	## Authored Disciplines (prose — internalize, don't cite)
87	
88	### Crash-fast, spelled per language
89	
90	The step body re-implements BCG's die-at-point-of-failure without BUK.
91	
92	**bash/sh** — an inline brace group to stderr, then `exit 1`, on every fallible
93	command and after every `test`:
94	
95	```bash
96	some_command \
97	  || { echo "FATAL: <what failed> — <why it matters>" >&2; exit 1; }
98	```
99	
100	**python** — the two-helper preamble that opens every python step:
101	
102	```python
103	def die(msg):
104	    print(f"FATAL: {msg}", file=sys.stderr)
105	    sys.exit(1)
106	
107	def require_env(name):
108	    val = os.environ.get(name, "")
109	    if not val:
110	        die(f"{name} missing")
111	    return val
112	```
113	
114	`die` is python's `buc_die`; `require_env` is the assert-presence discipline for
115	substitutions (CBi_101). Read required inputs through `require_env`; read
116	optional ones with `os.environ.get(name, "")` and an explicit absent-branch.
117	
118	When a python step shells out, use `subprocess.run(..., check=True)` so a
119	non-zero exit raises — unless a non-zero exit is itself the verdict (e.g. an
120	`openssl` signature check), in which case omit `check=True` and inspect
121	`returncode`, then `die` on the failing branch.
122	
123	### The Native-Serializer Rule
124	
125	Structured output is JSON. **Use the interpreter's native serializer when it is
126	free; hand-roll only to avoid pulling a tool into the builder container.** Same
127	rule everywhere, opposite mechanic by environment:
128	
129	- **python**: `json.dump` / `json.loads` — stdlib, free. Always.
130	- **host**: `jq -n --arg/--slurpfile` — the host has `jq`; never concatenate,
131	  never YAML (Cloud Build's wire format is JSON; RBSCJ).
132	- **bash, inside a step**: hand-rolled `printf`/concatenation, because pulling
133	  `jq` into a minimal skopeo container is friction — and permitted *only* because
134	  every interpolated value is controlled (hex digests, ISO timestamps, sanitized
135	  origins, SA emails), none of which can carry a literal quote:
136	
137	```bash
138	# No jq inside the step — values are controlled; none can carry a quote.
139	ENVELOPE='{'
140	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\","
141	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\",\"${FINGERPRINT}\"]"
142	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}"
143	```
144	
145	If a value could ever carry a quote or newline, it is not controlled — do not
146	hand-roll it.
147	
148	### Separate pure logic from network ops
149	
150	Resolve inputs, build paths, and author envelopes in a block with no network
151	calls; isolate `skopeo`/`gcloud`/`curl`/`urllib` calls in their own block. The
152	pure block is testable without the cloud; the network block can be stubbed.
153	BCG's visible-transformation philosophy applied to a step's internal structure.
154	
155	### Step-body skeleton
156	
157	**bash/sh**: a single file; first executable line `set -euo pipefail`; no module
158	header, kindle, or sentinel (BUK constructs absent in the builder). The shebang
159	is stamped by the assembler, not written by the author (CBh_101).
160	
161	**python**: stdlib only — no `pip install` in-step; the preamble above; `main()`
162	guarded by `if __name__ == "__main__":`.
163	
164	### GAR authentication idiom
165	
166	GAR reads/writes use the in-memory metadata token as credentials, never a
167	credential helper (the gcloud helper is unavailable to skopeo; rationale in
168	RBSCB). The token itself is fetched fresh per step — bash via the `token-fetch`
169	snippet (CBh_101), python via `urllib` against `metadata.google.internal`. skopeo
170	copies pass `--all` for multi-arch safety:
171	
172	```bash
173	skopeo copy --all "docker://${ORIGIN}" "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
174	  --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
175	  || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }
176	```
177	
178	The *invariant* that the token never reaches `/workspace` is CBi_102; this is the
179	*idiom* that honors it.
180	
181	---
182	
183	## Cited Rules (numbered — each has, or will have, a citer)
184	
185	Headers tag the rule: ❌ a failure mode, ✅ an established correct shape.
186	Families: `CBi_` cloud-step invariants (any language), `CBb_` bash/sh, `CBp_`
187	python, `CBh_` host-composition seam. Numbered from 101 to leave room for
188	insertions; once a rule has a citer it is never renumbered.
189	
190	### CBi — Cloud Step Invariants
191	
192	#### ✅ CBi_101: Substitutions are inputs — assert presence
193	
194	`_RBGx_*` values arrive as environment variables (submit-time expansion via
195	`automapSubstitutions`), not as arguments, and **may be empty**. Assert every
196	required input before use and die if absent; read optional inputs with an
197	explicit empty default and a documented absent-branch. The set a step requires is
198	declared in its header comment and enforced at dispatch (CBh_102).
199	
200	*Cited by:* step header contracts; the dispatch validator.
201	
202	#### ✅ CBi_102: The `/workspace` boundary — non-secret only, secrets step-local, guard every read
203	
204	`/workspace` is the inter-step bus. Write provenance envelopes, rosters, build
205	context — **never secrets**. Tokens and credentials stay step-local and
206	in-memory, re-minted per step (RBSCB records the canonical invariant). Every
207	consumer guards: the producing step may not have run or may have produced
208	nothing — test presence **and** non-emptiness, and die naming the missing
209	producer:
210	
211	```bash
212	test -f /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
213	  || { echo "FATAL: lode_stamps.txt not found — step 01 must run first" >&2; exit 1; }
214	test -s /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
215	  || { echo "FATAL: lode_stamps.txt is empty — nothing ensconced" >&2; exit 1; }
216	```
217	
218	*Cited by:* RBSCB; every `/workspace` guard and the GAR-auth idiom.
219	
220	#### ✅ CBi_103: Every state change is idempotent under retry
221	
222	Cloud Build may silently re-run a step. Inspect-or-create, never bare-create;
223	retag is a no-op if the tag exists; a second run reaches the same end state
224	without error. Exemplar — the `buildx-bootstrap` snippet: `inspect || create`,
225	then `use`.
226	
227	*Cited by:* the bootstrap snippet header; any retry post-mortem.
228	
229	#### ✅ CBi_104: Code to the builder image's pinned runtime
230	
231	The interpreter version is whatever the builder image ships — not your laptop's.
232	Guard any feature newer than the pinned runtime:
233	
234	```python
235	if sys.version_info >= (3, 12):
236	    tar.extractall(filter="data")   # filter= is 3.12+
237	else:
238	    tar.extractall()                # gcloud image ships 3.10
239	```
240	
241	*Cited by:* version-guard sites; a memo if a runtime mismatch bites.
242	
243	### CBb — Bash / sh Dialect
244	
245	#### ✅ CBb_101: Guarded `$( … )` with `pipefail` is permitted in-step — BCG's temp-file mandate is relaxed
246	
247	BCG bans pipelines inside `$()` and unguarded command substitution, mandating
248	temp files so each transformation is visible and recoverable. **Inside a step
249	body that mandate is relaxed:** temp-file forensics are low-value in an ephemeral
250	container whose stdout is already the build log, and `set -o pipefail` plus an
251	immediate presence check covers the failure surface.
252	
253	```bash
254	TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
255	test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "FATAL: no access_token in metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }
256	```
257	
258	The relaxation is **characterized, not careless**: it holds because (a) the log
259	is the forensic surface, (b) `pipefail` is set, and (c) the result is immediately
260	validated. Outside a step body, BCG's rule stands. This is the rule a reviewer
261	needs a handle for — a step comment can cite `CBb_101` to preempt "this violates
262	BCG."
263	
264	*Cited by:* step-body comments; code review.
265	
266	#### ✅ CBb_102: Capture substitutions into shell vars before snippets read them
267	
268	Substitutions are not shell variables you can loop over; capture them into plain
269	shell vars at the top of the body. Snippets (CBh_101) read these **plain shell
270	vars**, never the `_RBGx_` names — that is what lets one snippet serve callers in
271	disjoint substitution namespaces.
272	
273	```bash
274	SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
275	SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
276	```
277	
278	*Cited by:* the snippet contract (CBh_101).
279	
280	### CBp — Python Dialect
281	
282	#### ❌ CBp_101: The python helper preamble is duplicated — a known reuse gap
283	
284	The bash steps share a composed-snippet library (`rbgjs`, CBh_101); the python
285	steps do **not**. `die`, `require_env`, `metadata_token`, `gar_fetch`, and
286	`gar_json` appear **verbatim** in every python step (`rbgja01`, `rbgjv02`, …).
287	
288	This is a recorded asymmetry, not an endorsed pattern. Until a python-side
289	composition mechanism exists, keep the preamble **byte-identical** across steps
290	so it can be lifted into a shared snippet later without reconciliation; treat
291	divergence as a bug. Closing the gap (a python `#@`-include analogue) is out of
292	the capture-unification heat's scope — a future itch.
293	
294	*Cited by:* a review flagging preamble drift; the future itch that closes it.
295	*Removal condition:* delete this rule when python steps gain a shared preamble.
296	
297	### CBh — Host-Composition Seam (what a body is authored against)
298	
299	The author writes the body, not the shebang, the Build JSON, or the dispatch.
300	These rules describe the machinery a body must fit.
301	
302	#### ✅ CBh_101: Shared bash logic is spliced on the host via `#@rbgjs_include`
303	
304	Cloud Build has no native step reuse, so shared **bash** logic is composed
305	*before submit*. A step marks an include point; the host expander
306	(`zrbfc_expand_includes`, `rbfcb_BuildHost.sh`) replaces each marker with the
307	body of `rbgjs/rbgjs-<name>.sh`, shebang stripped. A marker-free body is rewritten
308	unchanged (safe to call on every step); a missing snippet crashes the expander —
309	no silent skip.
310	
311	```bash
312	#@rbgjs_include token-fetch
313	#@rbgjs_include gcrane-fingerprint
314	```
315	
316	**Snippet contract** (RBSCJ): snippets read **plain shell vars** set before the
317	marker (CBb_102), not `_RBGx_` substitutions, and are idempotent (CBi_103). The
318	library shares only the irreducibly identical core; kind-specific assembly stays
319	in the step.
320	
321	| Snippet | Requires | Provides |
322	|---------|----------|----------|
323	| `token-fetch` | none (ambient metadata) | `TOKEN` |
324	| `gcrane-fingerprint` | `ORIGIN`, `RAW_FILE` | `SHA`, `FINGERPRINT` |
325	| `gcrane-append` | `APPEND_CTX`, `APPEND_URI` | the FROM-scratch single-layer image pushed to `APPEND_URI` |
326	| `gpg-verify-sums` | `SUMS_URL`, `SIG_URL`, `TARGET_BASENAME`, `KEY_FPR` | `EXPECTED_SHA` |
327	| `buildx-bootstrap` | none | the `rb-builder` buildx builder |
328	| `buildx-push` | `PUSH_URI`, `PUSH_PLATFORMS`, `PUSH_CTX` | the image pushed |
329	
330	Bash-only today — see CBp_101 for the python gap. The recipe row's `entrypoint`
331	field (`bash`/`sh`/`busybox`/`python3`) selects the shebang the spine stamps; the
332	author declares the interpreter there (`busybox` → `#!/busybox/sh`, the only shell
333	in a distroless `:debug` builder such as `gcrane:debug`).
334	
335	*Cited by:* every `#@rbgjs_include` site; the snippet contract.
336	
337	#### ✅ CBh_102: The substitution blob is opaque to the spine; coverage is validated at dispatch
338	
339	A per-kind body builds the substitution blob (`jq -n` → `_RBGx_` keys) and hands
340	it to the spine, which passes it through without reading any individual key
341	(keeping the spine kind-agnostic). Before the expensive submit, the validator
342	scans each include-expanded step body for the `_RBGx_` tokens it references
343	(*requires*) and fails if any is absent from the blob's keys (*provides*) — the
344	heat's one declared behavior-add, whose reject path carries its own unit tests.
345	A body author's obligation: every `_RBGx_` the body reads must be a key the
346	kind's blob provides.
347	
348	*Cited by:* the validator's reject message; its unit tests.
349	
350	#### ✅ CBh_103: A step returns data to the host via `buildStepOutputs`
351	
352	`/workspace` does not survive the build. A step that must hand a value back writes
353	it to the `buildStepOutputs` channel (`/builder/outputs/output`); the spine
354	extracts the slot and base64-decodes it host-side into a capture file. This is
355	the only step→host return path.
356	
357	*Cited by:* the spine's extract logic; any step producing a host-consumed value.
358	
359	---
360	
361	## Snippet & Exemplar Reference
362	
363	Read these as the worked forms behind the rules above:
364	
365	- **Bash snippets** — `Tools/rbk/rbgjs/rbgjs-{token-fetch,gcrane-fingerprint,gcrane-append,gpg-verify-sums,buildx-bootstrap,buildx-push}.sh`
366	- **Bash steps** — `rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh`, `rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh`, `rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh`
367	- **Python steps** — `rbgja/rbgja01-discover-platforms.py`, `rbgja/rbgja03-build-info-per-platform.py`, `rbgjv/rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py`
368	- **Host composition** — `rblds_Spine.sh` (spine, validator, entrypoint switch, `buildStepOutputs` extract), `rbfca_StepAssembly.sh` (recipe rows), `rbfcb_BuildHost.sh` (`zrbfc_expand_includes`)
369	- **Per-kind body** — `rbldb_Bole.sh` (recipe + substitution blob + envelope intent)
370	
371	## Related Documents
372	
373	- **BCG** — Bash Console Guide. Host bash discipline; CBb_101 relaxes one of its rules in-step.
374	- **RCG** — Rust Coding Guide. Host Rust sibling.
375	- **WSG** — Windows Scripting Guide. Structural precedent: BCG's discipline re-expressed for a hostile foreign environment, catalogued as cited rules.
376	- **RBSCJ** — CloudBuildJson. JSON-composition trade study; home of the composed-snippet/contract decision CBh points at.
377	- **RBSCB** — CloudBuildPosture. skopeo-token/credential-helper posture and the canonical `/workspace`-no-secrets invariant (CBi_102).
378	
379	## Acronym Registry
380	
381	| Term | Expansion |
382	|------|-----------|
383	| CBG | Cloud Build Guide (this document) |
384	| Step body | The script run inside one Cloud Build step's builder container |
385	| Substitution | A `_RBGx_` value expanded at submit time, provided to the step as an env var |
386	| `/workspace` | The shared, ephemeral inter-step filesystem mount (non-secret only) |
387	| Snippet | A composed-once bash fragment spliced into a step via `#@rbgjs_include` |
388	| Spine | The host capture-assembly composer (`rblds_`) that submits and polls a build |

file.content:
<!--

SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
See LICENSE for terms.
-->

# Cloud Build Guide (CBG) — Step-Body Discipline

## Purpose

CBG codifies the discipline for authoring the **body of a single Google Cloud
Build step** — the script that runs inside one step's builder container. It is a
foreign-environment sibling to BCG (host bash) and RCG (host Rust): it shares
their philosophy — crash-fast, no silent failures, load-bearing complexity — but
diverges entirely on mechanics, because a step body executes in an environment
with none of the host kit's scaffolding.

CBG governs **single-step precision**: what goes inside one step's script. The
one seam it also covers is **how a step body is composed and dispatched** (the
include splice, the substitution contract, the return channel), because a body
must be authored *against* that machinery.

CBG is **polyglot**. A step body is bash, POSIX `sh`, or `python3` — the recipe
selects the interpreter per step. The cloud-step disciplines are
language-neutral; each language honors them with its own idiom.

## How this document is organized — two genres on purpose

CBG deliberately separates two kinds of knowledge, because they earn their form
differently:

- **Authored Disciplines** (prose, no IDs) — systematic, interderivable patterns
  for code *we* write. Like BCG, these are internalized, not cited by number;
  numbering them would be ceremony with no citers.
- **Cited Rules** (numbered `CB*-`) — discrete facts about the cloud Palisade we do
  *not* control (surprises, divergences, a recorded gap), each of which
  something will *point at*: a step comment justifying a workaround, a memo
  recording a probe, a review flagging a violation, or the dispatch validator.
  An ID earns its existence only when a citer will exist.

This split is itself an instance of the load-bearing test (BCG Core Philosophy):
catalog what is foreign; systematize what is yours.

## Core Philosophy

**The step body has no kit runtime.** On the host, BCG code leans on `buc_die`,
`buc_step`, the kindle/sentinel lifecycle, `buv_*` validation, and the module/CLI
gateway. **None of that exists inside a step.** A step body is a single file
shipped into a vendor builder container and run with a bare interpreter. Every
guarantee BCG gets from BUK, a step body must re-establish in a few lines of raw
bash or stdlib python. The philosophy survives the crossing; the mechanics are
rebuilt minimally, in-language. This is the relationship WSG has to BCG: the same
discipline, re-expressed for an environment BCG's helpers cannot reach.

**Crash-fast is non-negotiable, and louder here.** A step's stdout/stderr *is*
the Cloud Build log — the only forensic surface. There is no transcript file, no
`buc_log_*` sink, no temp directory to inspect afterward. A failure that does not
print and exit non-zero is a failure that vanishes. Every fallible operation dies
loudly, at its location, with a message naming what failed.

**The vendor is at the Palisade.** Cloud Build, the metadata server, the builder
images, and the substitution machinery are not ours to edit (Rules of
Engagement). We characterize their behavior precisely, contain our dependence at
named seams, and never paper over it. The Cited Rules below *are* that
characterization.

**Load-bearing complexity still rules.** Reuse only the irreducibly identical
core; leave kind-specific assembly in the step (RBSCJ composition rationale).

## The Cloud Step Environment

A step body runs under conditions a host script never faces. Each is a fault
domain — the source of most Cited Rules below.

| Condition | Consequence for the body |
|-----------|--------------------------|
| **Substitutions arrive as environment variables** (`_RBGx_*`, expanded at submit via `automapSubstitutions`). | Read from the environment, not argv; they may be empty (CBi_101). |
| **`/workspace` is a shared, ephemeral mount** seen by every step, destroyed at build end. | The only inter-step channel; non-secret by invariant; guard every read (CBi_102). |
| **Steps can be retried silently** on transient failure. | Every state change must be idempotent (CBi_103). |
| **Authentication is ambient**, via the metadata server. | Tokens are fetched in-memory, used, discarded — never to `/workspace` (CBi_102). |
| **The interpreter is the builder image's, pinned** (e.g. `gcloud` ships python 3.10). | Code to the shipped runtime; guard newer features (CBi_104). |
| **No native step reuse** (no anchors, no includes). | Shared bash logic is composed in on the host before submit (CBh_101). |

---

## Authored Disciplines (prose — internalize, don't cite)

### Crash-fast, spelled per language

The step body re-implements BCG's die-at-point-of-failure without BUK.

**bash/sh** — an inline brace group to stderr, then `exit 1`, on every fallible
command and after every `test`:

```bash
some_command \
  || { echo "FATAL: <what failed> — <why it matters>" >&2; exit 1; }
```

**python** — the two-helper preamble that opens every python step:

```python
def die(msg):
    print(f"FATAL: {msg}", file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(1)

def require_env(name):
    val = os.environ.get(name, "")
    if not val:
        die(f"{name} missing")
    return val
```

`die` is python's `buc_die`; `require_env` is the assert-presence discipline for
substitutions (CBi_101). Read required inputs through `require_env`; read
optional ones with `os.environ.get(name, "")` and an explicit absent-branch.

When a python step shells out, use `subprocess.run(..., check=True)` so a
non-zero exit raises — unless a non-zero exit is itself the verdict (e.g. an
`openssl` signature check), in which case omit `check=True` and inspect
`returncode`, then `die` on the failing branch.

### The Native-Serializer Rule

Structured output is JSON. **Use the interpreter's native serializer when it is
free; hand-roll only to avoid pulling a tool into the builder container.** Same
rule everywhere, opposite mechanic by environment:

- **python**: `json.dump` / `json.loads` — stdlib, free. Always.
- **host**: `jq -n --arg/--slurpfile` — the host has `jq`; never concatenate,
  never YAML (Cloud Build's wire format is JSON; RBSCJ).
- **bash, inside a step**: hand-rolled `printf`/concatenation, because pulling
  `jq` into a minimal skopeo container is friction — and permitted *only* because
  every interpolated value is controlled (hex digests, ISO timestamps, sanitized
  origins, SA emails), none of which can carry a literal quote:

```bash
# No jq inside the step — values are controlled; none can carry a quote.
ENVELOPE='{'
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\",\"${FINGERPRINT}\"]"
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}"
```

If a value could ever carry a quote or newline, it is not controlled — do not
hand-roll it.

### Separate pure logic from network ops

Resolve inputs, build paths, and author envelopes in a block with no network
calls; isolate `skopeo`/`gcloud`/`curl`/`urllib` calls in their own block. The
pure block is testable without the cloud; the network block can be stubbed.
BCG's visible-transformation philosophy applied to a step's internal structure.

### Step-body skeleton

**bash/sh**: a single file; first executable line `set -euo pipefail`; no module
header, kindle, or sentinel (BUK constructs absent in the builder). The shebang
is stamped by the assembler, not written by the author (CBh_101).

**python**: stdlib only — no `pip install` in-step; the preamble above; `main()`
guarded by `if __name__ == "__main__":`.

### GAR authentication idiom

GAR reads/writes use the in-memory metadata token as credentials, never a
credential helper (the gcloud helper is unavailable to skopeo; rationale in
RBSCB). The token itself is fetched fresh per step — bash via the `token-fetch`
snippet (CBh_101), python via `urllib` against `metadata.google.internal`. skopeo
copies pass `--all` for multi-arch safety:

```bash
skopeo copy --all "docker://${ORIGIN}" "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
  --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
  || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }
```

The *invariant* that the token never reaches `/workspace` is CBi_102; this is the
*idiom* that honors it.

---

## Cited Rules (numbered — each has, or will have, a citer)

Headers tag the rule: ❌ a failure mode, ✅ an established correct shape.
Families: `CBi_` cloud-step invariants (any language), `CBb_` bash/sh, `CBp_`
python, `CBh_` host-composition seam. Numbered from 101 to leave room for
insertions; once a rule has a citer it is never renumbered.

### CBi — Cloud Step Invariants

#### ✅ CBi_101: Substitutions are inputs — assert presence

`_RBGx_*` values arrive as environment variables (submit-time expansion via
`automapSubstitutions`), not as arguments, and **may be empty**. Assert every
required input before use and die if absent; read optional inputs with an
explicit empty default and a documented absent-branch. The set a step requires is
declared in its header comment and enforced at dispatch (CBh_102).

*Cited by:* step header contracts; the dispatch validator.

#### ✅ CBi_102: The `/workspace` boundary — non-secret only, secrets step-local, guard every read

`/workspace` is the inter-step bus. Write provenance envelopes, rosters, build
context — **never secrets**. Tokens and credentials stay step-local and
in-memory, re-minted per step (RBSCB records the canonical invariant). Every
consumer guards: the producing step may not have run or may have produced
nothing — test presence **and** non-emptiness, and die naming the missing
producer:

```bash
test -f /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
  || { echo "FATAL: lode_stamps.txt not found — step 01 must run first" >&2; exit 1; }
test -s /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
  || { echo "FATAL: lode_stamps.txt is empty — nothing ensconced" >&2; exit 1; }
```

*Cited by:* RBSCB; every `/workspace` guard and the GAR-auth idiom.

#### ✅ CBi_103: Every state change is idempotent under retry

Cloud Build may silently re-run a step. Inspect-or-create, never bare-create;
retag is a no-op if the tag exists; a second run reaches the same end state
without error. Exemplar — the `buildx-bootstrap` snippet: `inspect || create`,
then `use`.

*Cited by:* the bootstrap snippet header; any retry post-mortem.

#### ✅ CBi_104: Code to the builder image's pinned runtime

The interpreter version is whatever the builder image ships — not your laptop's.
Guard any feature newer than the pinned runtime:

```python
if sys.version_info >= (3, 12):
    tar.extractall(filter="data")   # filter= is 3.12+
else:
    tar.extractall()                # gcloud image ships 3.10
```

*Cited by:* version-guard sites; a memo if a runtime mismatch bites.

### CBb — Bash / sh Dialect

#### ✅ CBb_101: Guarded `$( … )` with `pipefail` is permitted in-step — BCG's temp-file mandate is relaxed

BCG bans pipelines inside `$()` and unguarded command substitution, mandating
temp files so each transformation is visible and recoverable. **Inside a step
body that mandate is relaxed:** temp-file forensics are low-value in an ephemeral
container whose stdout is already the build log, and `set -o pipefail` plus an
immediate presence check covers the failure surface.

```bash
TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "FATAL: no access_token in metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }
```

The relaxation is **characterized, not careless**: it holds because (a) the log
is the forensic surface, (b) `pipefail` is set, and (c) the result is immediately
validated. Outside a step body, BCG's rule stands. This is the rule a reviewer
needs a handle for — a step comment can cite `CBb_101` to preempt "this violates
BCG."

*Cited by:* step-body comments; code review.

#### ✅ CBb_102: Capture substitutions into shell vars before snippets read them

Substitutions are not shell variables you can loop over; capture them into plain
shell vars at the top of the body. Snippets (CBh_101) read these **plain shell
vars**, never the `_RBGx_` names — that is what lets one snippet serve callers in
disjoint substitution namespaces.

```bash
SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
```

*Cited by:* the snippet contract (CBh_101).

### CBp — Python Dialect

#### ❌ CBp_101: The python helper preamble is duplicated — a known reuse gap

The bash steps share a composed-snippet library (`rbgjs`, CBh_101); the python
steps do **not**. `die`, `require_env`, `metadata_token`, `gar_fetch`, and
`gar_json` appear **verbatim** in every python step (`rbgja01`, `rbgjv02`, …).

This is a recorded asymmetry, not an endorsed pattern. Until a python-side
composition mechanism exists, keep the preamble **byte-identical** across steps
so it can be lifted into a shared snippet later without reconciliation; treat
divergence as a bug. Closing the gap (a python `#@`-include analogue) is out of
the capture-unification heat's scope — a future itch.

*Cited by:* a review flagging preamble drift; the future itch that closes it.
*Removal condition:* delete this rule when python steps gain a shared preamble.

### CBh — Host-Composition Seam (what a body is authored against)

The author writes the body, not the shebang, the Build JSON, or the dispatch.
These rules describe the machinery a body must fit.

#### ✅ CBh_101: Shared bash logic is spliced on the host via `#@rbgjs_include`

Cloud Build has no native step reuse, so shared **bash** logic is composed
*before submit*. A step marks an include point; the host expander
(`zrbfc_expand_includes`, `rbfcb_BuildHost.sh`) replaces each marker with the
body of `rbgjs/rbgjs-<name>.sh`, shebang stripped. A marker-free body is rewritten
unchanged (safe to call on every step); a missing snippet crashes the expander —
no silent skip.

```bash
#@rbgjs_include token-fetch
#@rbgjs_include gcrane-fingerprint
```

**Snippet contract** (RBSCJ): snippets read **plain shell vars** set before the
marker (CBb_102), not `_RBGx_` substitutions, and are idempotent (CBi_103). The
library shares only the irreducibly identical core; kind-specific assembly stays
in the step.

| Snippet | Requires | Provides |
|---------|----------|----------|
| `token-fetch` | none (ambient metadata) | `TOKEN` |
| `gcrane-fingerprint` | `ORIGIN`, `RAW_FILE` | `SHA`, `FINGERPRINT` |
| `gcrane-append` | `APPEND_CTX`, `APPEND_URI` | the FROM-scratch single-layer image pushed to `APPEND_URI` |
| `gpg-verify-sums` | `SUMS_URL`, `SIG_URL`, `TARGET_BASENAME`, `KEY_FPR` | `EXPECTED_SHA` |
| `buildx-bootstrap` | none | the `rb-builder` buildx builder |
| `buildx-push` | `PUSH_URI`, `PUSH_PLATFORMS`, `PUSH_CTX` | the image pushed |

Bash-only today — see CBp_101 for the python gap. The recipe row's `entrypoint`
field (`bash`/`sh`/`busybox`/`python3`) selects the shebang the spine stamps; the
author declares the interpreter there (`busybox` → `#!/busybox/sh`, the only shell
in a distroless `:debug` builder such as `gcrane:debug`).

*Cited by:* every `#@rbgjs_include` site; the snippet contract.

#### ✅ CBh_102: The substitution blob is opaque to the spine; coverage is validated at dispatch

A per-kind body builds the substitution blob (`jq -n` → `_RBGx_` keys) and hands
it to the spine, which passes it through without reading any individual key
(keeping the spine kind-agnostic). Before the expensive submit, the validator
scans each include-expanded step body for the `_RBGx_` tokens it references
(*requires*) and fails if any is absent from the blob's keys (*provides*) — the
heat's one declared behavior-add, whose reject path carries its own unit tests.
A body author's obligation: every `_RBGx_` the body reads must be a key the
kind's blob provides.

*Cited by:* the validator's reject message; its unit tests.

#### ✅ CBh_103: A step returns data to the host via `buildStepOutputs`

`/workspace` does not survive the build. A step that must hand a value back writes
it to the `buildStepOutputs` channel (`/builder/outputs/output`); the spine
extracts the slot and base64-decodes it host-side into a capture file. This is
the only step→host return path.

*Cited by:* the spine's extract logic; any step producing a host-consumed value.

---

## Snippet & Exemplar Reference

Read these as the worked forms behind the rules above:

- **Bash snippets** — `Tools/rbk/rbgjs/rbgjs-{token-fetch,gcrane-fingerprint,gcrane-append,gpg-verify-sums,buildx-bootstrap,buildx-push}.sh`
- **Bash steps** — `rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh`, `rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh`, `rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh`
- **Python steps** — `rbgja/rbgja01-discover-platforms.py`, `rbgja/rbgja03-build-info-per-platform.py`, `rbgjv/rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py`
- **Host composition** — `rblds_Spine.sh` (spine, validator, entrypoint switch, `buildStepOutputs` extract), `rbfca_StepAssembly.sh` (recipe rows), `rbfcb_BuildHost.sh` (`zrbfc_expand_includes`)
- **Per-kind body** — `rbldb_Bole.sh` (recipe + substitution blob + envelope intent)

## Related Documents

- **BCG** — Bash Console Guide. Host bash discipline; CBb_101 relaxes one of its rules in-step.
- **RCG** — Rust Coding Guide. Host Rust sibling.
- **WSG** — Windows Scripting Guide. Structural precedent: BCG's discipline re-expressed for a hostile foreign environment, catalogued as cited rules.
- **RBSCJ** — CloudBuildJson. JSON-composition trade study; home of the composed-snippet/contract decision CBh points at.
- **RBSCB** — CloudBuildPosture. skopeo-token/credential-helper posture and the canonical `/workspace`-no-secrets invariant (CBi_102).

## Acronym Registry

| Term | Expansion |
|------|-----------|
| CBG | Cloud Build Guide (this document) |
| Step body | The script run inside one Cloud Build step's builder container |
| Substitution | A `_RBGx_` value expanded at submit time, provided to the step as an env var |
| `/workspace` | The shared, ephemeral inter-step filesystem mount (non-secret only) |
| Snippet | A composed-once bash fragment spliced into a step via `#@rbgjs_include` |
| Spine | The host capture-assembly composer (`rblds_`) that submits and polls a build |

#### ✅ CBi_105: A step uses only the tools its builder image ships — never install at build time

A body that `apt-get install`s or `pip install`s a tool pulls unpinned bytes from
a public repository into a privileged build at run time — a supply-chain hole no
digest pin can see, and friction every silent retry pays again. A missing tool is
a **builder-selection problem, not an installation problem**: move the work to a
step whose builder ships the tool (the JSON-work ↔ registry-work split across the
gcloud/gcrane builders is the worked form), or take another in-language path
(python stdlib instead of jq). The skeleton's "no `pip install`" is this rule's
python face; `apt-get install` is its bash face — and the bash face is the
sneakier one, because `apt-get` itself is allowlisted for the conformance walk.

*Cited by:* step reviews (first citer: the immure select-step draft that
apt-installed jq, reworked 2026-06-10); builder-selection notes in step headers.

### CBb — Bash / sh Dialect

#### ❌ CBp_102: Python bodies sit outside the conformance walk — hold the tool floor by hand

The supply-chain cupel walks `*.sh` only: a python step's imports and
`subprocess` calls are unscanned (live consequence: `rbgjv02` subprocess-runs
`gcloud`, a command the bash allowlist never sanctioned). Until a python walk
exists, authors hold the line manually:

- **Imports: stdlib only, from the established floor** (`base64`, `datetime`,
  `io`, `json`, `os`, `sys`, `tarfile`, `time`, `urllib`). A third-party import
  binds the step to the floating builder's unpinned pip set. Never
  `importlib`/`__import__`/`exec`/`eval` — dynamic import defeats any future
  static walk.
- **`subprocess` argv[0] honors the same tool floor as bash steps.** Shelling out
  to a command a bash step could not name is the same violation in a python
  costume. Prefer in-process `urllib`/REST over shelling out at all.

*Cited by:* memo-20260610-heat-BH-fable-recommendation-python-import-allowlist;
python-step reviews.
*Removal condition:* flip ✅ (and trim to the floor list) when the cupel gains
the python walk and these checks become mechanical.

`gcloud`, a command the bash allowlist never sanctioned). Until a python walk

- **Imports: stdlib only, from the established floor** (`base64`, `datetime`,
  `io`, `json`, `os`, `sys`, `tarfile`, `time`, `urllib`). A third-party import
  binds the step to the floating builder's unpinned pip set. Never
  `importlib`/`__import__`/`exec`/`eval` — dynamic import defeats any future
  static walk.
- **`subprocess` argv[0] honors the same tool floor as bash steps.** Shelling out
  to a command a bash step could not name is the same violation in a python
  costume. Prefer in-process `urllib`/REST over shelling out at all.

*Cited by:* memo-20260610-heat-BH-fable-recommendation-python-import-allowlist;
python-step reviews.
*Removal condition:* flip ✅ (and trim to the floor list) when the cupel gains
the python walk and these checks become mechanical.

content.text:
Committed as `b5c84874e`. (The warning shows Opus mid-conversion — `rbgjl07-immure-select.sh` deleted, `.py` born. The blueprint landed.)

**Deft improvements made** — four surgical insertions, house style preserved (new numbers, never renumbered; cited-by lines; removal conditions on gap rules):

1. **CBi_105** (✅, new): *a step uses only the tools its builder ships — never install at build time.* A missing tool is a builder-selection problem, not an installation problem. Notes the sneaky part: `apt-get` itself is allowlisted, so the conformance walk can't catch an `apt-get install jq` — the rule has teeth the cupel doesn't. First citer: today's immure draft rework.
2. **CBp_102** (❌, new): *python bodies sit outside the conformance walk — hold the tool floor by hand.* The stdlib import floor (enumerated), the dynamic-import ban, and the subprocess-honors-the-bash-allowlist rule, with rbgjv02's `gcloud` named as the live consequence. Removal condition: flips ✅ when the cupel gains the python walk. Genre-matched to CBp_101 (recorded gap, not endorsed pattern).
3. **CBh_103** gains one sentence: buildStepOutputs slots are addressed *by step index*, so reordering recipe rows silently shifts slots — pin N with a comment naming the producing step.
4. Exemplar list gains `rbgjl06-package-delete.py` as the convergence-delete exemplar — it's the precedent every recent design conversation cites, and it wasn't in the reference list.

**Recommended for your review — larger than deft, not done:**

- **A. Name the tool floor inside CBG itself.** The curated allowlist (`ZRBTDRU_GCB_ALLOWED`) is invisible in CBG — it lives only in cupel source. A step author reading CBG alone cannot learn that jq is forbidden, which I'd argue is the *root cause* of today's jq draft: the rule existed but was undiscoverable at authoring time. The fix is a short "The Tool Floor" passage in Core Philosophy or the Environment table, naming the allowlist as the single floor for all step languages and pointing at the cupel as its enforcement. Medium edit, touches CBG's framing — your call on placement.
- **B. The cupel python walk** (my memo's recommendation) — a code pace somewhere, probably this heat's tail or the next. Flips CBp_102 to ✅ and gives both new rules their mechanical citers.
- **C. Probe whether `zrbfc_expand_includes` is already language-blind.** The `#@rbgjs_include` marker is a `#` comment — valid python syntax. If the expander doesn't care about language, a `rbgjs` python-preamble snippet might Just Work, resolving CBp_101 (the preamble-duplication gap) nearly for free. Needs a ten-minute probe, not an assumption — the shebang-strip and splice mechanics may have bash-shaped edges.
- **D. Verify the buildStepOutputs size cap empirically, then write the number into CBh_103.** I deliberately didn't write "4KB" into the guide — I believe that's the limit but won't put an unverified number in canon. The 8-leaf native-family envelope (next pace) will approach whatever it is, so the verification has a natural home there.
- **E. The gcloud adjudication** from the memo: either add `gcloud` to the allowlist (sanctioning rbgjv02's subprocess use) or convert its provenance-describe call to REST urllib like every other step. Operator fork — the memo holds both options.

My lean, if you want it: A is the one that prevents the next incident rather than documenting this one; C is the cheapest possible win if the probe comes back clean. B and D ride naturally with existing pace structure; E can wait for the cupel python walk to force the question.

message.content: That looks wrong, an enumeration of actual filenames in CBG

That looks wrong, an enumeration of actual filenames in CBG... I pitch we remove it.  Thoughts?  See below for what I saw that bugged me.

A. I confess I don't recall the locale with the clearest statement of what tools are allowed, but there should be one.  Would you survey and advise before we create a potentially new copy that can go out of sync with future forgetting?

B. If you made a memo, that is good enough; I'll walk all the memos we made today after BH finishes for planning cleanups.  Do create or update the memo if you like given this exchange.

C. Good thing to note in the memo for when we plan this work, add it

D. I much prefer contract via API, not empirical finding: websearch for a strong reference that we then retain somewhere.

E. Seems right

Based on the search results, I found relevant documentation about Cloud Build's `buildStepOutputs` and the `/builder/outputs/output` size limit.

Cloud Build stores list of build step outputs produced by builder images when Cloud Builders write to `$BUILDER_OUTPUT/output`, but only the first 4KB of data is stored.

This is the key size limit for the `buildStepOutputs` field in Cloud Build: **4 kilobytes (4KB)** is the maximum amount of data that will be stored from the `/builder/outputs/output` file.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.new_string:
## Interaction with the standing builder-pin memo

memo-20260610-heat-BH-fable-recommendation-pin-delete-builder flags the floating builder under a
privileged identity; this memo is its step-body complement. Floating builder × unscanned imports
compound: the builder decides what `import requests` resolves to. Pinning bounds the supply;
the allowlist bounds the demand.

## Addenda from the 2026-06-10 CBG exchange (operator review of these recommendations)

- **Where the authoritative tool floor lives (surveyed):** the doc comment on
  `ZRBTDRU_GCB_ALLOWED` in `rbtdru_cupel.rs` is the single clearest statement — it carries the
  membership criterion (empirical per-container presence in the controlled builders, NOT kit
  portability), the jq-exclusion rationale, and the conformance purpose. No spec or guide copy
  exists, and none should be created: CBG should carry a **pointer** to that constant as the floor's
  home ("reference the home, don't recreate" — ACG), never a restated list. CBG's CBi_105/CBp_102
  currently reference the concept; the pointer-line addition awaits operator placement choice.
- **Why the unsanctioned `gcloud` never failed an audit (operator question):** vacuous pass — the
  cupel never opens `.py` files, so rbgjv02 was never scanned at all. The audit did not tolerate the
  violation; it never saw it. No additional mechanism is implicated beyond the gap this memo records.
- **Probe before building the python walk:** check whether `zrbfc_expand_includes`
  (rbfcb_BuildHost.sh) is already language-blind — the `#@rbgjs_include` marker is a `#` comment,
  valid python syntax, so a shared python preamble snippet (`die`/`require_env`/`metadata_token`/
  `gar_fetch`/`gar_json`) might splice with zero expander work, resolving CBG CBp_101 (the
  preamble-duplication gap) nearly for free. Ten-minute probe; do it when planning the walk pace —
  the shebang-strip and any bash-shaped assumptions in the expander are the things to check.
- **buildStepOutputs cap is API contract, not folklore:** the Build API's `results.buildStepOutputs`
  stores "only the first 4KB" of `$BUILDER_OUTPUT/output`, ordered by build step index
  (https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/build-config-file-schema; the cloudbuild v1 API model docs
  state the 4KB retention on BuildStep outputs). Now cited in CBG CBh_103. Relevant ceiling for the
  podvm native-family 8-leaf envelope.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Fable recommendation — python cloud steps need an import allowlist (and a subprocess policy)

Date: 2026-06-10
Status: recommendation, grounded in code (second Fable review pass); not yet acted on. Operator-prompted:
"should we create an equivalent of the bash command allowlist for python, anchored on imports?"

## Verdict: yes — and the gap is already carrying live traffic

The cupel's supply-chain conformance walks `*.sh` only (`zrbtdru_walk_sh`,
`ZRBTDRU_UNIVERSE_FILE_EXT` in rbtdru_cupel.rs). Four python cloud steps exist today —
`rbgja01-discover-platforms.py`, `rbgja03-build-info-per-platform.py`,
`rbgjl06-package-delete.py`, `rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py` — and sit entirely outside the
conformance surface. A `.py` step sidesteps the bash-command discipline by construction.

The live specimen proving the hole is not hypothetical: **rbgjv02 `subprocess.run`s `gcloud`** —
a command absent from `ZRBTDRU_GCB_ALLOWED` — invisibly to the cupel. The invocation itself may be
legitimate (the SLSA provenance `describe`), but it is *unreviewed by the mechanism*, which is the
whole point of having one. CBG already names the rule this would mechanize (CBi_104 "code to the
builder image's pinned runtime") and already has the `CBp_` python family with a known-gap marker
(CBp_101) — the import allowlist is the missing citer.

## Recommended shape

Extend the cupel with a `*.py` walk over the step directories and two checks:

1. **Import allowlist, anchored on the module root** of every `import X` / `from X import Y`.
   Current empirical floor (the union of all four steps): `base64, datetime, io, json, os, sys,
   tarfile, time, urllib`. Everything else flagged. `importlib`, `__import__`, `exec`, `eval`
   banned outright — dynamic import defeats static conformance. Third-party imports (`requests`
   et al.) are the real quarry: they bind a step to the floating builder's unpinned pip set, the
   exact drift class the bash allowlist exists to stop. Stdlib-only is the right floor.

2. **Subprocess policy — the bridge between the two universes.** `subprocess` lets a python step
   shell out past both disciplines. Either ban it in steps (python steps exist for in-process
   JSON/HTTP work; shelling out belongs in bash steps), or — since rbgjv02 uses it today — scan
   `subprocess.run([...])` argv[0] literals against the same `ZRBTDRU_GCB_ALLOWED` list: one tool
   floor, two languages. Under that option, `gcloud` needs explicit adjudication (add it to the
   allowlist, or convert rbgjv02's describe call to REST urllib like every other step).

## Interaction with the standing builder-pin memo

privileged identity; this memo is its step-body complement. Floating builder × unscanned imports
compound: the builder decides what `import requests` resolves to. Pinning bounds the supply;
the allowlist bounds the demand.

content.text:
Committed (`07a9d906c`). Point by point:

**The exemplar enumeration — you were right, and it's gone.** The filename list was drift-by-construction (proof: it was already missing rbgjl06 when I arrived, and rbgjl07.py was born mid-conversation). Replaced with directory-grain pointers — "the newest step in each language is usually the best exemplar" is the durable form. One deliberate keep: the **CBh_101 snippet table** also names files, but it carries the requires/provides *contract* per snippet and currently matches disk exactly — that's load-bearing content, not an index. It will need pruning when the skopeo-eviction pace kills `token-fetch`; that pace's hidden-scope list already tracks the snippet, so the table ages with the code rather than silently.

**A — survey verdict: the authoritative statement already exists and is good.** The doc comment on `ZRBTDRU_GCB_ALLOWED` in `rbtdru_cupel.rs` carries everything: the membership criterion (empirical per-container presence, deliberately *not* kit portability), the jq-exclusion rationale, and the conformance purpose. No second copy exists anywhere, and none should — my advice is CBG gains a one-line **pointer** to that constant as the floor's home (ACG's reference-the-home), never a restated list. A pointer can't go out of sync with future forgetting; only a copy can. I held off adding it pending your placement preference: Core Philosophy ("The vendor is at the Palisade" paragraph) or the Environment table both fit.

**B & C — memo updated** with the survey verdict, the expander probe note (the `#@rbgjs_include` marker is a `#` comment, valid python — if `zrbfc_expand_includes` is language-blind, a shared python preamble resolves CBp_101 nearly free; ten-minute probe when planning the walk pace), and the items below. Ready for your post-₣BH planning sweep.

**D — it's contract, not folklore: 4KB.** The Build API's `results.buildStepOutputs` stores "only the first 4KB" of `$BUILDER_OUTPUT/output`, ordered "corresponding to build step indices" — both halves now cited in CBh_103 (the index-ordering language also retroactively grounds my slot-pinning sentence). Retained in the memo with links. Sources: [Build API reference](https://developers.google.com/resources/api-libraries/documentation/cloudbuild/v1/python/latest/cloudbuild_v1.projects.builds.html), [Build config schema](https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/build-config-file-schema).

**E — no cause for concern, and here's the precise reason: the pass was vacuous.** The cupel never opens `.py` files at all — rbgjv02 wasn't audited-and-tolerated, it was never seen. There's no second mechanism quietly permitting `gcloud`; the gap this memo records is the complete explanation. (Recorded in the memo addenda so the planning sweep doesn't re-ask.)

message.content:
Please notch your changes now and critically review Opus's work from the other chat- I've pasted its terminal output below.  It thinks we can wrap this.  Make memos out of noncritical findings, but provide me a prompt with directions to opus for anything that seriously concerns you and I'll submit that to Opus before wrap.  Also, we had probably slate a pace at the very end of BH to step through all memos with today's timestamp (some will be about non RB issues and can be ignored) so that we can arrange paces for the ones we judge worth fixing.  Lets not judge yet though, that is what that terminal pace will be for.  Make that terminal pace require Fable, incidentally.  Hmm, I am now recalling there might already be a terminal pace- if so, consider adding this to that one, though I'm not terribly concerned if we have multiple distinct terminal paces.




⏺ Everything verified and clean — the pace is functionally complete. The depot holds only the two pre-existing
  bole Lodes (no stray podvm), and shellcheck is clean (212 files).

  ₢BHAAW (lode-podvm-immure) — complete, every "Done when" met

  Landed across four notches:

  ┌───────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Commit   │                                             What                                             │
  ├───────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 96818610b │ Capture vertical: constants, cloud steps, rbldv body, kindle, divine legend, zipper,         │
  │           │ tabtarget                                                                                    │
  ├───────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ c78b1aa15 │ Python rewrite of the select step (resolved the jq/allowlist collision — your steer)         │
  ├───────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 52fa5ccc  │ Spine ALLOW_LOOSE fix (live-found submission bug) + divine legend cosmetic                   │
  ├───────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 2339de4e1 │ Specs (RBSLI + RBS0 quoin + RBSPV promotion) + podvm-lifecycle fixture                       │
  └───────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Done-when checklist:
  - ✅ immure captures one family end-to-end — live-proven (vw260610095327, digests byte-identical to upstream)
  - ✅ podvm-lifecycle service fixture green live (immure → divine cohort → augur members+envelope → per-member
  jettison → banish → absent), registered in service + complete suites + RBTDRC_FIXTURES
  - ✅ divine legend carries vw/vn
  - ✅ RBSLI subdoc + RBS0 rbtgo_lode_immure quoin landed (covers both families)
  - ✅ RBSPV promoted out of FUTURE/ (crane→gcrane, RBSCB cited, ignite-VM marked superseded)

  Verification: build green · shellcheck clean (212) · fast green (151/0) · manual lifecycle + the fixture both
  green live · depot clean.

  Follow-ups I'd flag (not this pace's scope):
  - The FOLLOWING pace (per the docket cinch): podvm-native full curation + curated multi-platform set + refresh
  mode — podvm-native is wired with a 2-leaf default but fixture-unproven
  - Architect's flags: a line in rbw-di's egress check inventory for quay's blob-CDN redirect hosts; the
  buildStepOutputs ~4KB ceiling wants a check before native's 8-leaf envelope reuses the slot shape
  - Minor: the whole RBSL spec family (RBSLU/A/B/C/D/E/I) isn't catalogued in claude-rbk-acronyms.md — a
  family-wide tidy

  The work is done and verified, but I won't wrap on my own — ready to wrap ₢BHAAW? (And if you'd like, I can
  capture those follow-ups as itches first.)

✻ Cogitated for 22m 30s
